race.name: Ghost Elf
entries: {@i "We are the light that remembers the world."}

As a ghost elf, you are one of the few truly immortal beings, destined to live until violence or disease ends you. You are delicate and pale, your skin shimmering with a faint light in the dark where you dwell. Most ghost elves are recluses who have no patience for mortal affairs, finding comfort only beneath the ancient canopies that have sheltered their kin for millennia. Some, however, grow weary of endless solitude and venture forth to find meaning beyond the trees.

Ghost elves are the most reclusive of all elven subraces, dwelling only in the deepest ancient forests where the canopy is so thick that sunlight barely reaches the forest floor. Pale-skinned and silver-haired, they have adapted to life in perpetual twilight, their eyes taking on strange hues ranging from pale pink to deep indigo.

What truly sets the ghost elves apart from all other elves--and indeed all mortal races--is their true immortality. While other elves may live for centuries, they still eventually fade. The ghost elves, however, do not age after reaching maturity. They are the only elves who are truly immortal, their lives measured not in years but in epochs.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Dexterity score increases by 2, your Intelligence score increases by 1, and your Wisdom score increases by 1. However, your Constitution score decreases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Ghost elves do not age after reaching maturity around 100 years of age. They are truly immortal, unable to die of old age. This does not make them invulnerable--they can still be killed by violence, disease, or magical effects--but the passage of time has no effect on their vitality once they reach their prime.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Ghost elves tend toward isolation, often favoring neutral alignments that reflect their detached view of mortal affairs. Many are Neutral or Neutral Good, though some embrace the solitude of Neutral Evil or the freedom of Chaotic Neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Ghost elves are similar in stature to other elves, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall and slender. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Accustomed to life amid darkness, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Luminescent Skin
entries: Your pale skin emits a faint, ethereal glow. You shed dim light in a 5-foot radius. This light is magical and appears as a soft shimmer in shades of pale blue to white.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic cannot put you to sleep.

entries.name: Trance
entries: Like other elves, ghost elves do not sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through long practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.

entries.name: Ghost Elf Immortality
entries: You do not age and cannot die of old age.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Elvish.

race.name: Nesherim (Beneshite)
entries: You have been born where celestial and infernal bloodlines collide.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Constitution score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nesherim mature at the same rate as humans, but their lifespans vary wildly. The clash of their warring heritages suspends the passage of time within their bodies. Death comes not from age but from the inevitable moment when constitution and will fail against the ceaseless internal strife. Most live every year as if it were their last.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Your dual heritage pulls you toward both mercy and ambition. Some Nesherim favor strict laws, while others choose freedom from constraint.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nesherim stand between 5 and 6 feet tall, their builds ranging from lithe to powerfully corded. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Child of the Flames
entries: You have resistance to fire damage and radiant damage, but you are vulnerable to force damage; raw, unaligned magic disrupts the fragile equilibrium within you.

entries.name: Planar Duality
entries: Your body remains a contested battleground. You have advantage on death saving throws. Additionally, you can count yourself as either celestial or fiend (your choice each time) for the purpose of spells or abilities that detect or affect creature type.

entries.name: Confluence Brand
entries: As a bonus action, you ignite with fused infernal flame and celestial light for 1 minute. While the brand persists, your melee weapon attacks and unarmed strikes deal an extra 1d4 fire damage and an extra 1d4 radiant damage on a hit. At the end of each of your turns while the brand lasts, you take 1 fire damage and 1 radiant damage (this damage ignores resistance and immunity). Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Torch of Two Suns
entries: As a bonus action, you can kindle a paired flame of radiant and infernal light that sheds dim light in a 15-foot radius for 1 hour or until you dismiss it (no action required). Once during that hour, you can use an action to flare the torch. When you do, the light becomes bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet until the end of the current turn. Each creature in the bright light other than you takes 2d6 fire damage and 2d6 radiant damage. You then take 2d6 fire damage and 2d6 radiant damage (this damage ignores resistance and immunity), and the torch is extinguished. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Beneshite

race.name: Nesherim (Nephilite)
entries: Nesherim descendants of infernal-dominant bloodlines.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Intelligence score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nesherim mature at the same rate as humans, but their lifespans vary wildly. The clash of their warring heritages suspends the passage of time within their bodies. Death comes not from age but from the inevitable moment when constitution and will fail against the ceaseless internal strife. Most live every year as if it were their last.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Your heritage drives a strict rivalry between discipline and desire.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nesherim stand between 5 and 6 feet tall, their builds ranging from lithe to powerfully corded. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Child of the Flames
entries: You have resistance to fire damage and radiant damage, but you are vulnerable to force damage; raw, unaligned magic disrupts the fragile equilibrium within you.

entries.name: Planar Duality
entries: Your body remains a contested battleground. You have advantage on death saving throws. Additionally, you can count yourself as either celestial or fiend (your choice each time) for the purpose of spells or abilities that detect or affect creature type.

entries.name: Flames of Judgment
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack or spell that deals fire damage, you can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier). On a failed save, the target takes radiant damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus and sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much radiant damage and doesn't shed light. After the radiant damage is applied, you take fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus; this damage ignores resistance and immunity. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Hellward Step
entries: When you take fire damage from a creature you can see, you can use your reaction to teleport up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. The fire damage you take from that source is reduced by your proficiency bonus. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Nephilite

race.name: Nesherim (Seraphite)
entries: Nesherim whose lineage leans toward celestial fire and celestial duty.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Wisdom score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nesherim mature at the same rate as humans, but their lifespans vary wildly. The clash of their warring heritages suspends the passage of time within their bodies. Death comes not from age but from the inevitable moment when constitution and will fail against the ceaseless internal strife. Most live every year as if it were their last.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Your dual bloodline pulls you toward protective order and stern judgment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nesherim stand between 5 and 6 feet tall, their builds ranging from lithe to powerfully corded. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Child of the Flames
entries: You have resistance to fire damage and radiant damage, but you are vulnerable to force damage; raw, unaligned magic disrupts the fragile equilibrium within you.

entries.name: Planar Duality
entries: Your body remains a contested battleground. You have advantage on death saving throws. Additionally, you can count yourself as either celestial or fiend (your choice each time) for the purpose of spells or abilities that detect or affect creature type.

entries.name: Sanctifying Lights
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature you can see within 30 feet of you (including yourself). The target gains temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Crown of Atonement
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage from another creature, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage the target takes by twice your proficiency bonus (minimum 2). The attacker then takes fire damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus, and you take radiant damage equal to your proficiency bonus (this damage ignores resistance and immunity). Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Seraphite

race.name: Goliath (Ogre-Blooded)
entries: Your goliath character has certain characteristics in common with all other goliaths.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Constitution score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Goliaths have lifespans comparable to humans. They enter adulthood in their late teens and usually live less than a century.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Goliath society, with its clear roles and tasks, has a strong lawful bent. The goliath sense of fairness, balanced with an emphasis on self-sufficiency and personal accountability, pushes them toward neutrality.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Natural Athlete
entries: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill.

entries.name: Stone's Endurance
entries: You can focus yourself to occasionally shrug off injury. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Mountain Born
entries: You have resistance to cold damage. You're also acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 2 (instead of 1). Your Intelligence score decreases by 2, and your Wisdom score decreases by 2.

entries.name: Size
entries: Ogre-Blooded Goliaths are between 8 to 10 feet tall and weigh between 400 to 600 pounds. Your size is Large.

entries.name: Great Physique
entries: You gain +1 hit point per level. You have a +2 bonus to all Constitution checks and Strength checks.

entries.name: Oversized
entries: Due to your Large size, you cannot wear non-magical equipment designed for Medium or Smaller creatures. Equipment for your size costs 1.5x the original price, and refitting existing equipment will cost half its original price. You treat medium-sized weapons differently due to your large stature. Below is a list of trait changes when wielded by a Large creature.

items: Two-Handed becomes Versatile
Versatile becomes One-Handed
Anything else is considered an Improvised Weapon

race.name: Goliath (Troll-Blooded)
entries: Troll-blooded goliaths are a giant-blooded strain of goliath whose inherited troll vitality makes them larger and tougher than standard goliaths.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Constitution score increases by 2 instead of 1. Your Charisma score decreases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Goliaths have lifespans comparable to humans. They enter adulthood in their late teens and usually live less than a century.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Goliath society, with its clear roles and tasks, has a strong lawful bent. The goliath sense of fairness, balanced with an emphasis on self-sufficiency and personal accountability, pushes them toward neutrality.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Natural Athlete
entries: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill.

entries.name: Stone's Endurance

Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Mountain Born
entries: You have resistance to cold damage. You are also acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 2 instead of 1. Your Charisma score decreases by 1.

entries.name: Size
entries: Troll-blooded goliaths are between 8 and 10 feet tall and weigh between 400 and 600 pounds. Your size is Large.

entries.name: Troll-Blooded Regeneration
entries: You have advantage on death saving throws. When you become stable while at 0 hit points, you can choose to regain 1 hit point and become conscious instead of remaining unconscious. Once you regain hit points this way, you cannot use this trait again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Oversized
entries: Due to your Large size, you cannot wear non-magical equipment designed for Medium or smaller creatures. Equipment made for your size costs 1.5x the original price, and refitting existing equipment costs half its original price. You also treat Medium weapons differently due to your large stature:

items: Two-Handed becomes Versatile
Versatile becomes One-Handed
Anything else is considered an Improvised Weapon

race.name: Onihan (Oni-zu)
entries: Onihans have certain traits derived from their oni ancestry.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Intelligence score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Onihan mature at the same rate as humans but live considerably longer due to their giant heritage, often reaching 200 years or more.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Onihan are not predisposed to any particular alignment. Their dual nature--both mortal and mystical--means they can embrace any moral philosophy. However, their experiences being viewed as omens sometimes pushes them toward neutrality.

entries.name: Size
entries: Onihan are slightly taller and more solidly built than typical humans, ranging from 5 1/2 to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Thanks to your oni heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as though it were bright light, and in darkness as though it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Oni Resilience
entries: Your oni blood knits wounds, but in measured ways. When you spend Hit Dice during a short rest, add your proficiency bonus to one of those dice. In addition, once per short rest, as an action, you can spend one Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + your Constitution modifier. This use doesn't benefit from features that add to healing from Hit Dice.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Oni-zu

entries.name: Oni-zu
entries: Oni-zu are descended from oni mages, the most common and magically adept oni. Your skin bears shades of blue or green, and you possess slender, traditional oni horns that curve gracefully from your forehead. You inherit your ancestors' innate magical cunning and powers of deception.

entries.name: Cunning of Night
entries: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made in dim light or darkness, and you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed.

entries.name: Oni Heritage Magic
entries: You learn one cantrip of your choice from {@spell minor illusion} or {@spell thaumaturgy}. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast {@spell disguise self} once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast {@spell darkness} once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

race.name: Onihan (Go-zu)
entries: Onihans have certain traits derived from their oni ancestry.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Strength score increases by 1.

In addition, once per short rest, as an action, you can spend one Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + your Constitution modifier. This use doesn't benefit from features that add to healing from Hit Dice.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Go-zu

entries.name: Go-zu
entries: Go-zu are descended from the bull-headed oni who served as soldiers and guardians in celestial armies. Your skin ranges from grey to orange to purple, and you bear thick, bull-like horns that curve outward or upward, though still refined and petite compared to true oni. You inherit your ancestors' physical might and indomitable presence.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Charging Strike
entries: When you move at least 10 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee weapon attack on the same turn, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier). On a failed save, the target is pushed up to 10 feet away from you. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Bull-Headed
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed.

entries.name: Gatekeeper's Insight
entries: You can cast {@spell detect evil and good} once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

race.name: Onihan (Me-zu)
entries: Onihans have certain traits derived from their oni ancestry.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Dexterity score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Onihan mature at the same rate as humans but live considerably longer due to their giant heritage, often reaching 200 years or more.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Onihan are not predisposed to any particular alignment. Their dual nature--both mortal and mystical--means they can embrace any moral philosophy. However, their experiences being viewed as omens sometimes pushes them toward neutrality.

entries.name: Size
entries: Onihan are slightly taller and more solidly built than typical humans, ranging from 5 1/2 to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 35 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Thanks to your oni heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as though it were bright light, and in darkness as though it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Oni Resilience
entries: Your oni blood knits wounds, but in measured ways. When you spend Hit Dice during a short rest, add your proficiency bonus to one of those dice. In addition, once per short rest, as an action, you can spend one Hit Die to regain hit points equal to the roll + your Constitution modifier. This use doesn't benefit from features that add to healing from Hit Dice.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Me-zu

entries.name: Me-zu
entries: Me-zu are descended from the horse-headed oni who served as commanders and special operatives within celestial courts. Your skin bears earthen tones ranging from brown to beige, and you possess very small horns--sometimes mere nubs. Wild, mane-like hair grows from the crown of your head, but you are naturally unable to grow hair on the sides of your head, creating a distinctive hair pattern. You inherit your ancestors' supernatural mobility and tactical poise.

entries.name: Fleet of Foot
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Messenger's Agility
entries: When you take the Dash action, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement until the end of your turn. Additionally, when you take the Dash action, you can make a single melee weapon attack or shove a creature as a bonus action.

entries.name: Supernatural Leap
entries: Your long jump distance is doubled, and your high jump distance is tripled. Additionally, you can use this trait to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your level.

entries.name: Oni Heritage Magic
entries: You learn one cantrip of your choice from {@spell guidance} or {@spell blade ward}. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast {@spell longstrider} on yourself once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

race.name: Vaetyr (Hearthbound)
entries: Emerging from the icy crags and hidden inlets of secluded their lands, the Vaetyr blend the hardiness of dwarves with the ingenuity of gnomes, all while harboring a unique connection to the sea and the ancient magic of their forebears. Their society, built upon the pillars of rune-craft and hearth-magic, thrives in frigid, isolated realms where they navigate treacherous ice-flows with the skill of seasoned mariners.

entries.name: Hardy and Wise
entries: Vaetyr are as sturdy as the mountains from which they partly descend, combined with the keen intellect of their gnome heritage. They are shorter and stockier than humans, but their compact stature belies their resilience and mental acuity. Their skin often bears the pallor of the icy landscapes they call home, with hair colors ranging from deep browns to light, frost-touched hues.

entries.name: Secluded Mariners
entries: Despite their isolation, Vaetyr are adept seafarers, navigating the icy waters that surround their homelands with ease. This dichotomy of seclusion and exploration marks their unique place in the world -- a race of skilled mariners who seldom leave the cold embrace of their frozen realm.

entries.name: Mystical Crafters
entries: The Vaetyr's culture is steeped in the mystical arts of Hearth Magic and Rune Forging. Hearthbound Vaetyr are the keepers of their communities, using their magic to protect and nurture, while Runebound Vaetyr are the crafters and defenders, imbuing objects with magical runes of great power.

entries.name: Vaetyr Names
entries: A blend of their dual heritage, Vaetyr names reflect both the sturdy traditions of dwarves and the whimsical creativity of gnomes. Their names are often inspired by various elements of their rich culture, from their seafaring exploits and rune crafting skills to their deep connection with hearth magic and the diverse landscapes they inhabit.

In Vaetyr culture, clan names hold significant meaning, serving as a testament to a family's ancestral legacy. These names are often passed down through generations, each one symbolizing a specific aspect of their heritage, be it their seafaring prowess, mastery of runecraft, or their unwavering connection to the hearth's warmth. A Vaetyr's clan name is not just a label; it's a reflection of their identity and a source of pride within their close-knit communities.
Male Names: Berend, Brorim, Dornick, Ebermar, Eldar, Fimlin, Garvik, Grilbin, Jorvik, Keldorin, Noran, Norix, Rurik, Thelbin, Tholmin, Ulbor, Varnik, Wilfryn, Zanrik
Female Names: Adwyna, Berylla, Brynja, Eldeth, Elyndra, Firla, Gwinna, Jasmik, Karyn, Lindryn, Lyrin, Morna, Nitrana, Sylvi, Thalia, Tylira, Vistra, Wistala, Ysmera
Clan Names: Frostfall, Wavecarver, Runescribe, Emberheart, Deepdelve, Glimmerstone, Seabreach, Windwhisper, Hearthguard, Tidewalker

entries.name: Whispers on the Wind
entries: In the isolated reaches where the Vaetyr make their homes, encounters with other races are rare and often marked by the icy veil of their secluded society. Their interactions are shaped by the rhythms of the sea and the solemnity of the frozen landscapes they inhabit. Here are some Vaetyr perspectives on other races, whispered like secrets on the wind:

**Humans:** "Quick as a summer gale, humans come and go, their lives a whirlwind of fleeting moments. Their ceaseless ambition and impulsive actions often bewilder us. While we admire their fervor, their haste contrasts sharply with our measured ways."

**Elves:** "Elves, with their timeless forests, speak a language of nature akin to our own. Yet, their woodlands sing a different tune from our coastal shores. We share a dance of respect at a distance, their eternal elegance a stark contrast to the warmth of our hearths."

**Dwarves:** "Sturdy as the stone, our dwarf kin delve deep into the earth, much like our own connection to the sea. We share a bond of craftsmanship and resilience, though our artistic expressions differ. In their company, we find a familiar stubbornness, a reliable if unyielding alliance."

**Gnomes:** "Gnomes, our inventive kin, ignite sparks of creativity that brighten our lives. Their vibrant spirits bring a warmth that stirs the still air of our solitude. Though we tread different paths, the echoes of our shared laughter resonate in the tranquility of our gomes."

**Halflings:** "Rare as a bloom in snow, halflings bring joy to our lands. Their resilience, cloaked in cheer, is a reminder of life's warmth in the harshest climes. Their visits, though fleeting, leave footprints of happiness in our hearts. We cherish their fleeting presence by the hearth."

**Dragonborn:** "Dragonborn, with their elemental essence, command respect like a fierce storm. Their strength, a contrast to our serenity, sparks admiration, a kinship rooted in the primal forces of nature. We value their tenacity and power, knowing the importance of balance in our world."

**Tieflings:** "In tieflings, we see reflections of our own misunderstood nature. Judged by their appearance, they find a semblance of acceptance with us. Their tales, woven with isolation and inner conflict, resonate with the silent songs of our hearths. We share the burden of misjudgment."

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 2, your Intelligence score increases by 1, your Wisdom score increases by 1, your Dexterity score decreases by 2, and your Charisma score decreases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Vaetyr reach adulthood at the same age as humans, but they're considered young until they reach the age of 45. On average, they live about 400 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Vaetyr are smaller than humans, averaging around 4 to 4.5 feet in height. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 25 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Thanks to your gnome and dwarf ancestry, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Seafaring Heritage
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Survival} skill and {@item Navigator's Tools|phb|navigator's tools}.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Hearthbound

entries.name: Hearthbound
entries: As a Hearthbound Vaetyr, you have a deep connection to the sanctuary of home and the magic that protects and nurtures those within it. Your abilities grow as you do, reflecting the ever-strengthening bond with your hearth.

entries.name: Hearth Magic
entries: An innate Vaetyr magical ability, Hearth Magic draws upon their spiritual connection to home and hearth to form minor magic. You gain the following Hearth Magic Ability:
Enchanted Embers.

entries.name: Enchanted Embers
entries: You create a magical flame that provides warmth and light in a 10-foot radius, lasting for 8 hours or until you leave. The flame is a symbol of comfort and safety within your home or camp.

entries.name: Rune Forging
entries: A blend of innate magical potential and centuries-old traditions, Rune Forging is a skill that allows the Vaetyr to craft intricate runes imbued with versatile enchantments. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch an object, and you inscribe a different rune onto each of the objects. To be eligible, an object must be a weapon, a suit of armor, a shield, a piece of jewelry, or something else you can wear or hold in a hand. Your rune remains on an object until you finish a long rest, and an object can bear only one of your runes at a time. You learn the following rune:

entries.name: Fortitude Rune
entries: This rune grants resilience and strength. When inscribed, it imbues the wearer with temporary hit points. The wearer gains 1d4 temporary hit points for each point of their proficiency bonus, bolstering their fortitude in the face of adversity.

entries.name: Potent Hearth Magic
entries: You are gifted with a profound connection to the magic of the hearth, which manifests in several potent abilities that grow in strength with the Vaetyr. These abilities are deeply rooted in the traditions of nurturing and protection, vital to the well-being of their communities. At their stated levels, you learn the following Hearth Magic Abilities:

entries.name: Private Paradise (Level 3)
entries: You can create a comforting illusion within your home or camp (within a 30 foot cube), lasting 8 hours or until you or any allies leave the area. It can make your sanctuary appear as a natural and serene part of the surroundings, concealing you and your allies within. Sound from within the illusion does not escape outside. If a creature other than you or your allies walks into the illusion, the ability fails.

entries.name: Vitality's Vigor (Level 5)
entries: You can designate a creature sharing your hearth. After rest, the creature gains temporary hit points equal to your character level and a skill check bonus equal to half your character level (rounded down) for 8 hours.

entries.name: Restful Respite (Level 7)
entries: You can designate a creature sharing your hearth. After rest, the creature loses an extra level of exhaustion and receives the effects of Lesser Restoration.

entries.name: Second Sanctuary (Level 10)
entries: You gain the ability to designate a short rest area as "home" for 1 hour, enabling the use of your Hearth Magic abilities with reduced potency. 8 hour durations are reduced to 1 hour and Restful Respite may only remove a level of exhaustion or provide the benefits of Lesser Restoration, not both.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common, Dwarvish, and Gnomish.

race.name: Vaetyr (Runebound)
entries: Emerging from the icy crags and hidden inlets of secluded their lands, the Vaetyr blend the hardiness of dwarves with the ingenuity of gnomes, all while harboring a unique connection to the sea and the ancient magic of their forebears. Their society, built upon the pillars of rune-craft and hearth-magic, thrives in frigid, isolated realms where they navigate treacherous ice-flows with the skill of seasoned mariners.

entries.name: Hardy and Wise
entries: Vaetyr are as sturdy as the mountains from which they partly descend, combined with the keen intellect of their gnome heritage. They are shorter and stockier than humans, but their compact stature belies their resilience and mental acuity. Their skin often bears the pallor of the icy landscapes they call home, with hair colors ranging from deep browns to light, frost-touched hues.

entries.name: Secluded Mariners
entries: Despite their isolation, Vaetyr are adept seafarers, navigating the icy waters that surround their homelands with ease. This dichotomy of seclusion and exploration marks their unique place in the world -- a race of skilled mariners who seldom leave the cold embrace of their frozen realm.

entries.name: Mystical Crafters
entries: The Vaetyr's culture is steeped in the mystical arts of Hearth Magic and Rune Forging. Hearthbound Vaetyr are the keepers of their communities, using their magic to protect and nurture, while Runebound Vaetyr are the crafters and defenders, imbuing objects with magical runes of great power.

entries.name: Vaetyr Names
entries: A blend of their dual heritage, Vaetyr names reflect both the sturdy traditions of dwarves and the whimsical creativity of gnomes. Their names are often inspired by various elements of their rich culture, from their seafaring exploits and rune crafting skills to their deep connection with hearth magic and the diverse landscapes they inhabit.

In Vaetyr culture, clan names hold significant meaning, serving as a testament to a family's ancestral legacy. These names are often passed down through generations, each one symbolizing a specific aspect of their heritage, be it their seafaring prowess, mastery of runecraft, or their unwavering connection to the hearth's warmth. A Vaetyr's clan name is not just a label; it's a reflection of their identity and a source of pride within their close-knit communities.
Male Names: Berend, Brorim, Dornick, Ebermar, Eldar, Fimlin, Garvik, Grilbin, Jorvik, Keldorin, Noran, Norix, Rurik, Thelbin, Tholmin, Ulbor, Varnik, Wilfryn, Zanrik
Female Names: Adwyna, Berylla, Brynja, Eldeth, Elyndra, Firla, Gwinna, Jasmik, Karyn, Lindryn, Lyrin, Morna, Nitrana, Sylvi, Thalia, Tylira, Vistra, Wistala, Ysmera
Clan Names: Frostfall, Wavecarver, Runescribe, Emberheart, Deepdelve, Glimmerstone, Seabreach, Windwhisper, Hearthguard, Tidewalker

entries.name: Whispers on the Wind
entries: In the isolated reaches where the Vaetyr make their homes, encounters with other races are rare and often marked by the icy veil of their secluded society. Their interactions are shaped by the rhythms of the sea and the solemnity of the frozen landscapes they inhabit. Here are some Vaetyr perspectives on other races, whispered like secrets on the wind:

**Humans:** "Quick as a summer gale, humans come and go, their lives a whirlwind of fleeting moments. Their ceaseless ambition and impulsive actions often bewilder us. While we admire their fervor, their haste contrasts sharply with our measured ways."

**Elves:** "Elves, with their timeless forests, speak a language of nature akin to our own. Yet, their woodlands sing a different tune from our coastal shores. We share a dance of respect at a distance, their eternal elegance a stark contrast to the warmth of our hearths."

**Dwarves:** "Sturdy as the stone, our dwarf kin delve deep into the earth, much like our own connection to the sea. We share a bond of craftsmanship and resilience, though our artistic expressions differ. In their company, we find a familiar stubbornness, a reliable if unyielding alliance."

**Gnomes:** "Gnomes, our inventive kin, ignite sparks of creativity that brighten our lives. Their vibrant spirits bring a warmth that stirs the still air of our solitude. Though we tread different paths, the echoes of our shared laughter resonate in the tranquility of our gomes."

**Halflings:** "Rare as a bloom in snow, halflings bring joy to our lands. Their resilience, cloaked in cheer, is a reminder of life's warmth in the harshest climes. Their visits, though fleeting, leave footprints of happiness in our hearts. We cherish their fleeting presence by the hearth."

**Dragonborn:** "Dragonborn, with their elemental essence, command respect like a fierce storm. Their strength, a contrast to our serenity, sparks admiration, a kinship rooted in the primal forces of nature. We value their tenacity and power, knowing the importance of balance in our world."

**Tieflings:** "In tieflings, we see reflections of our own misunderstood nature. Judged by their appearance, they find a semblance of acceptance with us. Their tales, woven with isolation and inner conflict, resonate with the silent songs of our hearths. We share the burden of misjudgment."

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 2, your Intelligence score increases by 1, your Wisdom score increases by 1, your Dexterity score decreases by 2, and your Charisma score decreases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Vaetyr reach adulthood at the same age as humans, but they're considered young until they reach the age of 45. On average, they live about 400 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Vaetyr are smaller than humans, averaging around 4 to 4.5 feet in height. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 25 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Thanks to your gnome and dwarf ancestry, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Seafaring Heritage
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Survival} skill and {@item Navigator's Tools|phb|navigator's tools}.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Runebound

entries.name: Runebound
entries: As a Runebound Vaetyr, your affinity for rune magic is unparalleled. Your skills in inscribing ancient runes grant you and your allies enhanced abilities and protections, showcasing the depth of your rune-crafting heritage.

entries.name: Hearth Magic
entries: An innate Vaetyr magical ability, Hearth Magic draws upon their spiritual connection to home and hearth to form minor magic. You gain the following Hearth Magic Ability:
Enchanted Embers.

entries.name: Enchanted Embers
entries: You create a magical flame that provides warmth and light in a 10-foot radius, lasting for 8 hours or until you leave. The flame is a symbol of comfort and safety within your home or camp.

entries.name: Rune Forging
entries: A blend of innate magical potential and centuries-old traditions, Rune Forging is a skill that allows the Vaetyr to craft intricate runes imbued with versatile enchantments. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch an object, and you inscribe a different rune onto each of the objects. To be eligible, an object must be a weapon, a suit of armor, a shield, a piece of jewelry, or something else you can wear or hold in a hand. Your rune remains on an object until you finish a long rest, and an object can bear only one of your runes at a time. You learn the following rune:

entries.name: Fortitude Rune
entries: This rune grants resilience and strength. When inscribed, it imbues the wearer with temporary hit points. The wearer gains 1d4 temporary hit points for each point of their proficiency bonus, bolstering their fortitude in the face of adversity.

entries.name: Advanced Rune Forging
entries: You possess an innate talent for inscribing magical runes. Your abilities, unlocking as you grow, imbue objects with protective and enhancing powers.

entries.name: Clarity Rune (Level 3)
entries: This rune offers clarity of senses and mind. When inscribed, it bestows advantage on saving throws against being blinded, deafened, or stunned. Additionally, it enhances these saving throws with a bonus equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded down.

entries.name: Vigor Rune (Level 5)
entries: This rune embodies the vitality of the wilds. When applied to a weapon, it allows the wielder to regain hit points equal to their proficiency bonus on a successful hit. This rejuvenating power can be harnessed once per combat.

entries.name: Guardian Rune (Level 7)
entries: This rune channels the steadfastness of the earth. When inscribed on armor or clothing, it fortifies the wearer, granting a bonus to Armor Class equal to half of your proficiency bonus. This protective power is usable once per long rest.

entries.name: Augmentation Rune (Level 10)
entries: This rune is a testament to the art of rune mastery. Inscribed alongside another rune, it magnifies its power. Choose a rune you've already inscribed; this chosen rune is then augmented with enhanced capabilities. You may invoke this augmented power once per long rest, limited to augmenting one rune at a time.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common, Dwarvish, and Gnomish.

race.name: Wyrmblooded
entries: Slender yet majestic, Wyrmblooded are unique beings born from the rare unions between Wyrm and humanoids. They inherit traits from both lineages, possessing humanoid forms with the distinctive features of their Wyrm parentage.

Wyrmblooded are slender yet majestic, with physical features that betray their dual heritage. Their scales, horns, or other Wyrm-like features emerge in subtle or prominent ways depending on their Wyrm ancestry. Their eyes often carry an unnatural luminescence, and their presence carries an aura of ancient power.

entries.name: Ability Score Changes
entries: Your Constitution score decreases by 2, your Intelligence score decreases by 1, your Wisdom score decreases by 1, and your Charisma score decreases by 1. You choose one ability score from Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma to increase by 2. This ability change follows the race's Ability Score Improvement advancement.

entries.name: Age
entries: Wyrmblooded mature at the same rate as their humanoid parentage but live approximately three times longer due to their Wyrm heritage.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Their Wyrm lineage inclines Wyrmblooded towards chaotic alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Similar in size to their humanoid parent, Wyrmblooded fall into the same size category as their non-Wyrm lineage.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is based on your humanoid parentage.

entries.name: Wyrm Parentage
entries: You inherit specific essence, breath effect, and enhanced resistance based on your Wyrm parent's type. Choose one from the following:

items: Cinder -- Essence: Smoke and fire. Breath effect: Dexterity saving throw. The breath deals fire damage. On a failed save, the area becomes lightly obscured by smoke until the start of your next turn. Resistance: Fire (use as a reaction against one attack or effect).

Frost -- Essence: Ice and water. Breath effect: Dexterity saving throw. The breath deals cold damage. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone. Resistance: Cold (use as a reaction against one attack or effect).

Sand -- Essence: Earth and wind. Breath effect: Dexterity saving throw. The breath deals bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. On a failed save, the affected area becomes difficult terrain for a number of rounds equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up. Resistance: Bludgeoning, Slashing, and Piercing (use as a reaction against one attack or effect, choose the type when using the reaction).

Storm -- Essence: Lightning and thunder. Breath effect: Constitution saving throw. The breath deals thunder damage. On a failed save, the creature is stunned until the start of your next turn. Resistance: Lightning (use as a reaction against one attack or effect).

Blight -- Essence: Acid and poison. Breath effect: Constitution saving throw. The breath deals acid damage. On a failed save, the creature is poisoned until the start of your next turn. Resistance: Poison (use as a reaction against one attack or effect).

Chaos -- Essence: Wild magic. Breath effect: Charisma saving throw. The breath deals force damage. If at least one creature fails its saving throw, roll a d20. On a 1, a Wild Magic Surge occurs, centered on the breath's area or affecting one failed target, at the DM's discretion. Resistance: Force (use as a reaction against one attack or effect).

entries.name: Essence Breath
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive Wyrm essence into a 10-foot-radius circle centered on a point 5 feet directly in front of you, corresponding to your Wyrm parentage. Each creature in the area must make the saving throw determined by your Wyrm parentage. The DC for this saving throw is 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus.

On a failed save, a creature takes the breath's full damage and suffers its rider effect. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and suffers no rider effect. You deal 2d4 damage for every proficiency bonus you possess. You are immune to your own Essence Breath and its effects.
Lingering environmental effects created by Essence Breath last for a number of rounds equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up, unless the parentage effect states otherwise.

entries.name: Gestalt
entries: Learn from two classes simultaneously. When selecting one class, you may select another. You have access to your second class's features as you gain levels as if you were that class. However:

items: You may only choose half the skills for each of your classes (rounded down, minimum 1).
You gain half of the spell slots from each class (rounded down, minimum 1 of each level).
You gain half of any resource pool from each class (rounded down, minimum 1).
You suffer a -1 penalty to your proficiency bonus, which only increases at levels 5, 10, 15, and 20.

entries.name: Same Class, Different Subclass
entries: With the DM's permission, your gestalt path can be the same class as your primary class, provided you choose a different subclass, archetype, patron, origin, oath, circle, rite, or similar class specialization.

When you choose the same class twice, you use a single base class chassis. You do not gain that class's Hit Dice, proficiencies, starting equipment, spellcasting progression, pact magic progression, Ability Score Improvements, Extra Attack, class resources, or other base class features a second time.
Instead, when your class level grants a subclass feature, you gain the corresponding subclass feature from each of your chosen subclasses.
If both subclasses grant the same named feature, spell, resource, or option, the duplicate does not stack unless the DM determines the features are distinct.
Uses of subclass features draw from your normal class resources unless the feature creates its own separate pool.

entries.name: Lineage Form
entries: Form: Alter appearance to resemble your humanoid parentage's race. Cast {@spell disguise self} once per long rest, limited to your humanoid parentage's race.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common, Draconic, and the language of your humanoid parentage.

tools: one type of {@item artisan's tools|phb} of your choice

classFeature.name: Your Token
entries: As an occultist, you craft a personal, magical object known as a Token. A Token is an item with deep ties to your past or the Ethereal Plane, such as the belongings of a deceased loved one or a long-lost artifact.
Creating a Token takes 10 minutes, during which you inscribe or renew occult runes upon the object. Your Token serves as your spellcasting focus for your occultist spells while you are holding it.

You can have only one Token at a time. If you create a new Token, the previous object immediately loses its occult connection, though the object itself is not destroyed. This connection is bound to you; another creature cannot use your Token as a spellcasting focus.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You know four 1st-level occultist spells of your choice, with additional spells gained according to the Occultist table.
The Occultist Spellcasting table shows how many spells you know and your spell slots.
When you gain a level in this class, you can replace one known spell with another occultist spell of the same level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your occultist spells, since your magic is an expression of innate power and spiritual connection.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability modifier for occultist spell save DCs and spell attack rolls.
{@b Spell save DC} = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
{@b Spell attack modifier} = your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier

classFeature.name: Ritualist
entries: You can cast occultist spells you know as rituals if they have the ritual tag.

When you finish a long rest, you can attune one ritual occultist spell from the occultist spell list to your Token. The spell can be any ritual spell you do not need to know, but it must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
While that spell remains attuned, you can cast it as a ritual even if you do not know it. You cannot cast that spell with a spell slot unless you know it.
You can have only one attuned ritual spell at a time.

classFeature.name: Spectral Awareness
entries: Your connection to the Ethereal Plane allows you to passively sense when the boundary between the Material Plane and the Ethereal Plane is weak at your current location.
You also sense spirits and creatures tied to the Ethereal Plane within 60 feet of you. This sense tells you that such a presence is nearby, but not exact number, identity, or nature.
You have advantage on ability checks made to identify the nature or general characteristics of a spirit, creature, or weakened planar boundary you can sense.

classFeature.name: Occultist Rites
entries: At 3rd level, you choose an Occultist Rite. It grants features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

entries.caption: Occultist Rites
rows: Form covenants with spirits, ghosts, and ethereal entities.
Read and impose fate through curses, portents, and dark omens.
Bind and command ethereal servants and minions.

classFeature.name: Occultist Rites
entries: At 6th level, you gain the next feature from the Occultist Rite you chose at 3rd level.

classFeature.name: Occultist Rites
entries: At 10th level, you gain the next feature from the Occultist Rite you chose at 3rd level.

classFeature.name: Occultist Rites
entries: At 14th level, you gain the next feature from the Occultist Rite you chose at 3rd level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can’t increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can’t increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can’t increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can’t increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Master of the Grey
entries: At 18th level, your senses pierce the boundary between the Material Plane and the Border Ethereal. You can see and hear both planes within 60 feet of you.

As an action, choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you, or one unattended object or contiguous section of an object or structure that you can see within 60 feet of you and that fits within a 10-foot cube. You shift the target between the Material Plane and the Border Ethereal for 1 minute. An unwilling creature that succeeds on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC is unaffected.

A shifted target exists only on its new plane for the duration. An unwilling creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, returning to the plane it left on a success. You can end the effect early as a bonus action.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can’t increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Eternal Spirit
entries: When you would die, you can choose to pass on normally or anchor your spirit to your body or to the place where you died.

While anchored, your spirit exists in the Border Ethereal. You can choose to pass on at any time. You cannot cast spells or take actions except to attempt possession. As an action, you can target one creature you can see within 60 feet of your anchor. The target must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, which it can choose to fail. On a failure, you possess the target's body. On a success, the target is immune to this feature for 24 hours.

While possessing a creature, you control its body. You retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, personality, memories, spellcasting, class features, and proficiencies, but otherwise use the possessed creature's statistics. The possessed creature is aware of its surroundings but cannot act.

The possession lasts until the body drops to 0 hit points, you end it as an action, you are forced out by magic, or your original body is restored to life. When the possession ends, your spirit returns to your anchor unless your original body has been restored to life, in which case you return to it.
Once you anchor your spirit with this feature, you cannot do so again until your soul returns to a living body and you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Mana Capacity
entries: Your body can store up to 10 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus mana.

Your maximum mana capacity is determined after you finish a long rest; temporary changes to your Constitution modifier, such as from your Mana Enhancement feature or other magical effects, do not alter your maximum mana until you complete your next long rest.

classFeature.name: Mana Crystal
entries: Your training centers around a specially prepared crystal focus that serves as both your spellcasting focus and a reservoir for your magical energy.

entries.name: Crystal Charges
entries: The Mana Crystal has a maximum number of charges equal to your current maximum mana capacity. When you finish a long rest, the crystal regains all expended charges.

entries.name: Crystal Properties
entries: The crystal's appearance reflects your philosophical balance, typically featuring patterns that represent harmony between mind and body. The crystal glows softly when containing charges, with intensity reflecting available charge capacity. If lost or destroyed, you can craft a replacement during a long rest using materials worth 50 gp.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Your ability to absorb and manipulate magical energy allows you to cast spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your thaumaphage spells. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.
{@b Spell save DC} = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
{@b Spell attack bonus} = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

entries.name: Learning Spells
entries: Your ability to cast spells beyond the {@spell Convert Essence|VeiledOmens} cantrip and the {@spell Ether Burn|VeiledOmens} spell depends on the Devouring Art you choose at 2nd level. Some arts grant you methods to learn and cast a variety of spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know the {@spell Convert Essence|VeiledOmens} cantrip.

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: At 1st level, you know the {@spell Ether Burn|VeiledOmens} spell.

entries.name: Direct Casting
entries: You cast spells by expending mana directly. You do not use spell slots.
You can cast spells at higher levels by paying the mana cost of the higher level.

entries.name: Overexertion
entries: The Thaumaphage table shows your Mana Exertion Limit, the maximum amount of mana you can spend on a single spell without risk. If you cast a spell by expending an amount of mana that exceeds this limit, immediately after the spell is cast you must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to 10 + the amount of mana spent over your limit. On a failed save, you gain one level of exhaustion.

classFeature.name: Siphon Magic
entries: Your study of thaumic absorption allows you to draw out and internalize the magical energy stored within charged objects.

As an action, you can siphon energy from a magic item you are holding or wearing that uses charges. When you do so, you expend a number of charges from the item and regain a corresponding amount of mana.

items: {@b Mana Crystal:} When siphoning from your own Mana Crystal, you regain 1 mana for each charge you expend from the crystal.
{@b Other Magic Items:} When siphoning from any other magic item, you regain 1 mana for every 2 charges you expend, rounded down.

entries: You can siphon a maximum number of charges at one time equal to your proficiency bonus. The mana you regain cannot exceed your maximum mana capacity.

classFeature.name: Devouring Arts
entries: At 2nd level, you choose an art that shapes how you process and utilize absorbed magical energy. These specialized disciplines dictate how you metabolize, transform, and master the potent magical energies you take in, shaping them to your will.
Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.

entries.caption: Devouring Arts
rows: Absorbed mana becomes learned spell knowledge.
Absorbed magical energy becomes enhanced bodily capability.

classFeature.name: Devouring Arts
entries: At 6th level, you gain the next feature from the Devouring Art you chose at 2nd level.

classFeature.name: Devouring Arts
entries: At 10th level, you gain the next feature from the Devouring Art you chose at 2nd level.

classFeature.name: Devouring Arts
entries: At 14th level, you gain the next feature from the Devouring Art you chose at 2nd level.

classFeature.name: Consume Magic
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your mastery over absorption deepens, allowing you to intercept and devour hostile magical effects.

When you are targeted by a spell or other magical effect, you can use your reaction to attempt to negate and absorb it. The magical effect is nullified for you, and you regain mana equal to your proficiency bonus.
You can use this ability twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Mana Shift
entries: Starting at 17th level, your attunement to magical energies allows you to briefly become intangible, letting hostile magic pass through you.

When you would be affected by an instantaneous magical effect, such as a {@spell fireball} spell, a {@spell hold person} spell, or a dragon's breath weapon, you can use your reaction and expend 5 mana to momentarily phase into an incorporeal state. If you do so, the triggering instantaneous magical effect fails to affect you.

classFeature.name: True Devourer
entries: At 20th level, you achieve perfect harmony with the flow of magic, able to draw upon its deepest currents and perceive its true nature. You gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Empowered Mana:} Your maximum mana capacity increases by an amount equal to your Wisdom modifier. Additionally, when you roll initiative and have no mana remaining, you regain mana equal to your Wisdom modifier, minimum 1.

{@b Thaumic Sight:} You can use an action to activate this sight for 1 minute. While active, you gain truesight out to a range of 60 feet. Additionally, you can see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears magic, and you learn its school of magic, if any. You can discern the presence of magical traps or hidden magical effects within this range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Rite of Haunts
entries: The Rite of Haunts is for those Occultists who serve as conduits between the living and the dead. They form covenants with bound spirits, commune with the restless dead, and walk the threshold of the Border Ethereal. Through ritual practice, they learn not only to host a single spirit's power, but to read the haunting of places and people, release souls trapped by unfinished business, and guide the dead between worlds.

subclassFeature.name: Pact of Incantation
entries: You have learned a ritual that allows you to form a pact with a spirit. Over the course of 1 hour, you perform the ritual in the presence of a spirit, ghost, haunt, incorporeal undead, or other Ethereal remnant you can perceive, or at a location, object, corpse, or threshold to which such a spirit is bound.

If the spirit is willing, the ritual succeeds automatically. If the spirit is unwilling, it must remain within the ritual area for the full hour and fail a Charisma saving throw against your occultist spell save DC at the end of the ritual. Performing this ritual on an unwilling spirit is a violation; the spirit becomes hostile to you whether the ritual succeeds or fails, and remembers the violation.

When the ritual succeeds, you bind yourself to the spirit in a mutual pact. The spirit is not destroyed, controlled, or made into your servant. Choose one spirit from the Spirit Incantation table. You gain that spirit's 3rd-level incantation.

You can use your spirit incantations a combined number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. If an incantation calls for a saving throw, the DC equals your occultist spell save DC. An incantation is an occultist feature, not a spell, and it does not require spell components unless it modifies a spell you cast. When you use an incantation, your bound spirit collaborates in the effect rather than merely lending you power. Unless an incantation says otherwise, you can use no more than one spirit incantation on the same turn.
If you later release this spirit and form a new Pact of Incantation, choose a new spirit from the Spirit Incantation table. You retain no incantations from previously bound spirits.

You can end your pact through a 1-hour release ritual. If the spirit is unwilling to be released, or if you form a new pact without first releasing your bound spirit, the pact breaks. The spirit becomes hostile to you, manifesting in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you using an appropriate stat block chosen by the DM. A spirit released this way recognizes you on sight, ignoring nonmagical disguise and any effect that would conceal your identity, such as {@spell disguise self} or {@spell alter self}.

entries.name: Veil Step
entries: As a bonus action, you become spectral until the end of the current turn. During this time, you can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks, and you can move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain.
If you end your turn inside a creature or object, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take 1d10 force damage.

entries.name: Grave Hunger
entries: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can call on your bound wraith to drain the target's vitality. The target takes extra necrotic damage equal to 1d6 + your Charisma modifier, and you gain temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt by this incantation.

entries.name: Mourning Glimpse
entries: Once per turn, when one creature fails a saving throw against an occultist spell you cast, your bound banshee shows it a glimpse of deathly grief. The creature takes psychic damage equal to 1d6 + your Charisma modifier, and it cannot regain hit points until the start of your next turn.
If the failed saving throw caused the creature to be frightened, the creature also cannot take reactions until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Unseen Hand
entries: Immediately after you deal damage to a creature, you can force that creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you push the creature up to 10 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice.
If this movement causes the creature to strike an object, a structure, or another creature, the target takes force damage equal to your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Corpse-Light
entries: Immediately after you deal psychic, necrotic, or lightning damage to one creature, you can wreath the creature in corpse-light until the start of your next turn.

While wreathed in this way, the creature sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius, cannot benefit from being invisible against you, and the next attack roll made against it before the start of your next turn has advantage.

entries.name: Withering Dark
entries: Immediately after you deal necrotic damage to one creature, you can force that creature to make a Strength saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature has disadvantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws until the start of your next turn, its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn, and the first time it deals damage with an attack before the start of your next turn, that damage is reduced by 1d6 + your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Mediumship
entries: You learn three rituals of mediumship, each requiring 10 minutes to perform. You can use these rituals a combined number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
A use of any ritual is expended when the ritual is performed, regardless of outcome.

entries.name: Seance
entries: You touch a corpse, object, or place marked by death, violence, or strong emotion, or you call upon a spirit you can perceive. The spirit becomes audible and visible to you, even if it would normally be invisible, incorporeal, or on the Border Ethereal. You may ask the spirit up to five questions over the next 10 minutes.

If the spirit is willing, it answers truthfully. An unwilling spirit makes a Charisma saving throw against your occultist spell save DC. On a failure, it is compelled to answer truthfully. On a success, it may lie, refuse to answer, or end the ritual.
The spirit can answer only what it knew in life or has perceived since death, and it may answer cryptically if a direct answer would be incomplete or misleading.

entries.name: Bear Witness
entries: You touch a corpse, object, or place marked by death, violence, or strong emotion. The spiritual residue replays the strongest impression of an event before you as a vision lasting up to 1 minute.

The vision can reveal sights, sounds, words, visible magic, emotional residue, spiritual presences, and the apparent actions of creatures involved, as they were impressed upon the corpse, object, or place. The vision does not reveal thoughts, motives, secrets, or facts that left no spiritual impression. The DM determines what the vision depicts.

entries.name: Final Passage
entries: You perform a release ritual for a spirit bound to a location, object, corpse, or creature you can touch. If you correctly name or symbolically resolve the unfinished business that anchors the spirit, the spirit is peacefully released, its haunt ends, and the anchor no longer draws or sustains spirits.

The DM determines what counts as resolution, which may require role-played dialogue, the return of an item, the speaking of a name, or another action appropriate to the spirit's nature. An unwilling spirit may make a Charisma saving throw against your occultist spell save DC; on a success, the ritual fails and the spirit is alerted to your attempt. Final Passage does not affect your bound spirit.

subclassFeature.name: Covenant of Incantation
entries: Your Pact of Incantation deepens into a covenant. You gain the 10th-level incantation of the spirit you chose from the Spirit Incantation table.

entries.name: Borrowed Flesh
entries: As an action, choose one Humanoid within 5 feet of you. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. If a spell or other effect prevents the target from being possessed, this incantation fails against it. On a failed save, your bound ghost leaves you, its primary vessel, and enters the target's body for up to 1 minute. The target is incapacitated and loses control of its body, but remains aware of its surroundings.

While possessing the target, the ghost controls the body as an ally of you and your companions. The DM controls the possessed body. The ghost uses the target's statistics, except it retains its own alignment, personality, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. It can use the target's actions and traits, including Spellcasting or Innate Spellcasting, using the spell slots, uses, spellcasting ability, spell save DC, and spell attack bonus specified in the target's stat block. The ghost does not gain access to the target's knowledge, class features, legendary actions, lair actions, or mythic actions.

The possession lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if the possessed body drops to 0 hit points, if you end it as a bonus action, if you use another incantation, if you are incapacitated, or if the ghost is forced out by magic such as {@spell dispel evil and good}. When the possession ends, your bound ghost returns to you. On a successful initial save, the target is immune to this incantation for 24 hours.

entries.name: Gravebound Servant
entries: When a Humanoid you can see dies within 30 feet of you, or as an action when you touch the corpse of a Humanoid that died within the last minute, you can draw up its departing spirit. The spirit rises in the corpse's space or the nearest unoccupied space as a specter. The specter lasts for 1 minute, until it drops to 0 hit points, or until you use this incantation again.

The specter is friendly to you and your allies. It acts immediately after your turn and obeys your verbal commands, which require no action from you. If you issue no command, it takes the Dodge action. It uses the specter stat block, except it gains temporary hit points equal to half your occultist level, gains a bonus to its attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier, and cannot create spawn or cause another spirit to rise.

entries.name: Death Knell
entries: As an action, you release a deathly keening that turns fear into grief. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you that can hear you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d8 psychic damage and is frightened of you until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and is not frightened.
If a creature is already frightened of you when it fails this saving throw, it takes an extra 2d8 psychic damage, cannot take reactions, and its speed becomes 0 until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Spiteful Repulsion
entries: As an action, choose one creature or loose object you can see within 30 feet of you. An object must be Large or smaller, must weigh no more than 500 pounds, and must not be worn or carried.

A creature must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 4d8 force damage, you move it up to 30 feet horizontally to an unoccupied space you can see, and you can knock it prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and is not moved or knocked prone.

If you choose an object, you move it up to 30 feet horizontally toward an unoccupied space you can see. If the object would enter a creature's space during this movement, the object's movement ends and that creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. The creature takes 4d8 bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The object then falls in an unoccupied space of the DM's choice within 5 feet of the creature.

entries.name: Mortal Draught
entries: When a creature that is not a Construct or Undead drops to 0 hit points within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to draw in its fading spark. You regain hit points equal to 3d8 + your Charisma modifier, or you gain temporary hit points equal to that amount. You can then become invisible until the end of your next turn. This invisibility ends early immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.

If the creature dies before the start of your next turn, you regain one expended use of your spirit incantations. Once you regain a use in this way, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Umbral Cloak
entries: As a bonus action while you are in dim light or darkness, you wrap yourself in your bound shadow for 1 minute. The effect ends early if you are incapacitated or if you end it as a bonus action.

For the duration, you can take the Hide action as a bonus action while in dim light or darkness. In addition, at the start of each of your turns, if you are in dim light or darkness, you can become invisible until the start of your next turn. This invisibility ends early if you make an attack roll, cast a spell, or enter bright light.

subclassFeature.name: Psychopomp
entries: You are recognized by the restless dead as one who keeps the road between life and death. When a spirit, haunt, or incorporeal undead starts its turn within 30 feet of you, or enters that area for the first time on a turn, it must make a Charisma saving throw against your occultist spell save DC. On a failed save, it recognizes your authority as a psychopomp for 1 minute.

While a creature recognizes your authority in this way, it cannot attack you or target you with harmful abilities or magical effects, and you have advantage on ability checks you make to interact socially with it. The creature is not compelled to obey you, and this recognition does not prevent it from attacking your allies, fleeing, concealing information, or pursuing its own purpose.

On a successful save, the creature is immune to this feature for 24 hours. The recognition ends early if you or your allies damage the creature, or if you attempt to bind, command, or release it against its will.

In addition, when you use an incantation that has a range, target, area, or triggering distance measured from you, you can briefly send your bound spirit across the threshold. Choose an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you. Your bound spirit manifests there only long enough to deliver the incantation, and the incantation's range, target, area, or triggering distance is measured from that space instead of yours. The spirit then immediately returns to you.

You must still take the incantation's normal action, bonus action, or reaction and expend any use of the incantation. If the incantation requires a target, the target must be valid from the chosen space.

subclassFeature.name: Rite of Omens
entries: The Rite of Omens is for Occultists who read the residue of coming events where it gathers in the Border Ethereal. To them, fate leaves pressure before it arrives: in death echoes, spirit traces, old curses, fearful places, broken oaths, planar bleeds, and haunt-like impressions caught between the living world and the dead. Their power lies not merely in harming enemies, but in recognizing the patterns of fate that cling to all things and choosing how to meet them, bear them, or bind others to the doom already gathering around them.

subclassFeature.name: Rite Spells
entries: You learn the following spells at the listed occultist levels. They count as occultist spells for you and do not count against your spells known. These spells are your omen spells. If you already know one of these spells, choose another occultist spell of the same level from the divination, enchantment, or necromancy schools.

subclassFeature.name: Cast the Lots
entries: You prepare a set of divinatory implements for this rite. Your implement set can be marked bones, teeth, coins, knucklebones, rune-scraps, cards, lots, carved sticks, shells, or other objects that suit your practice. Each piece is marked to catch spirit traces, death echoes, and other pressures gathering in the Border Ethereal before they break into the present.

You use your divinatory implements for your Cast the Lots feature. When you create or renew your Token, you can choose your divinatory implements as the object that becomes your Token. If you do, the set counts as one object for the purpose of your Token feature.

When you finish a long rest, cast the lots with your divinatory implements and roll a d6, d8, d10, and d12 together, then record the results. These dice form your foretold portent, which lasts until you finish your next long rest.
Each die determines part of the portent. The d6 determines the subject, the d8 determines the trigger, the d10 determines the sign, and the d12 determines the shadow.

The portent never invokes itself.
