When your portent's trigger occurs within 60 feet of you and you can see or otherwise perceive it, you can use your reaction to invoke the portent, speaking or otherwise manifesting the cast words. When you do, apply the sign to the portent's subject. Once invoked, the portent is lost, unless a feature says otherwise.

Some Rite of Omens features refer to your imposed fate effects. An imposed fate effect is an occultist spell or Rite of Omens feature you create that curses, hexes, marks, seals, burdens, or binds a creature's fate, such as {@spell bane}, {@spell hex}, {@spell bestow curse}, {@spell geas}, {@spell Malediction|VeiledOmens}, {@spell Doom|VeiledOmens}, or a feature from this subclass.

The dice also form a cryptic phrase in this order: subject, trigger, sign, shadow. The phrase is read as "[Subject] [Trigger] [Sign], [Shadow]," such as "The Crown Rises Ashen, or Falters." The full reading includes the shadow, but only the subject, trigger, and sign are invoked when you use the portent. Speaking or otherwise manifesting the shadow has no mechanical effect unless the portent curdles.

entries.caption: d6: Subject
rows: One creature you can see within 30 feet of the triggering creature
The triggering creature and one other creature you can see within 30 feet of it
The creature nearest to the triggering creature, other than itself

entries: If the trigger does not involve a creature, you are the triggering creature for the purpose of determining the subject. If a result names more than one subject, each named creature is a subject of the portent. A sign applies to each subject it can affect. If a sign modifies a single triggering roll or event, it modifies that roll or event only once.

entries.caption: d8: Trigger
rows: A creature makes an attack roll.
A creature makes an ability check.
A creature makes a saving throw.
A creature takes or deals damage.
A creature moves or is moved.
A creature casts a spell or is targeted by a spell or magical effect.
A curse, charm, fear, possession, or imposed fate effect begins, ends, or is challenged.
A creature speaks a meaningful truth, lie, oath, threat, or confession.

entries.caption: d10: Sign
rows: Favor. Add your proficiency bonus to a triggering d20 roll made by a subject. If no subject made a triggering d20 roll, add your proficiency bonus to the subject's next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw before the start of your next turn.

Misfortune. Subtract your proficiency bonus from a triggering d20 roll made by a subject. If no subject made a triggering d20 roll, subtract your proficiency bonus from the subject's next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw before the start of your next turn.

Shelter. Reduce damage the subject takes from the trigger by your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. If the subject took no damage from the trigger, the subject gains temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier.
Ruin. The subject takes necrotic damage equal to your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier.

Revelation. You learn one useful true detail about the subject, the trigger, or the scene. The DM chooses the detail, which can include a hidden condition, a relevant resistance or vulnerability, a spiritual attachment, the presence of a curse or possession, a likely immediate danger, or the broad meaning of an omen.
Binding. The subject's speed becomes 0 and it cannot teleport or willingly move to another plane until the start of your next turn.

Severance. The next saving throw or ability check made before the start of your next turn to end, resist, or suppress a curse, charm, fear, possession, spell, or imposed fate effect affecting the subject gains or loses your proficiency bonus, your choice.
Burden. The subject cannot take reactions until the start of your next turn, and the next time it willingly moves before then, its speed is reduced by 10 feet for that movement.
Turning. Move the subject up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. An unwilling subject can make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC to resist this movement.
Withering. Until the start of your next turn, the subject cannot regain hit points, and any temporary hit points it would gain are reduced by your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier.

entries: The Shadow is the curdled consequence. It is read in the phrase, but it only takes effect if the omen curdles.

entries.caption: d12: Shadow
rows: Subtract your proficiency bonus from initiative rolls.
Your speed is reduced by 10 feet.
You cannot gain temporary hit points.
You have disadvantage on Constitution saving throws you make to maintain concentration.
When you regain hit points, reduce the hit points regained by your proficiency bonus, once per turn.
You shed dim, ghostly light in a 5-foot radius and cannot benefit from being invisible.
You cannot take reactions until after your first turn in each combat.
You have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks.
You cannot take the Dash action.
The first time on each of your turns that you deal damage to a creature, you take necrotic damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
You have disadvantage on Wisdom (Insight) and Charisma (Persuasion) checks.
You cannot take the Help action, and you cannot benefit from the Help action.

entries: If you finish a long rest while your foretold portent has not been invoked, the omen curdles and is lost. You do not make a new foretold portent at the end of that long rest. The curdled omen shadows you until you finish your next long rest, after which you can use Cast the Lots again. While the curdled omen shadows you, you suffer the curdled consequence shown by the d12.

subclassFeature.name: Signs in the Wake
entries: Your spells disturb the spiritual residue that clings to fate and leave signs in their passing. When you cast an occultist spell using a spell slot, you can choose one creature targeted by the spell, affected by the spell, or perceived by you through the spell. You learn one of the following facts about that creature, your choice:

items: One damage resistance, immunity, or vulnerability it has, if any.
One condition currently affecting it.
Its highest or lowest saving throw modifier, your choice.
Whether it is cursed, possessed, charmed, frightened, or under one of your imposed fate effects.
Whether it is below half its hit point maximum.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Second Reading
entries: You learn that fate may be fixed while its signs remain difficult to read. After you make your foretold portent, choose a number of the portent dice up to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up, and roll another die of the same size for each chosen die. Record both results for each chosen die.

When your portent is invoked or when it curdles, choose which recorded result applies for each chosen die. The chosen results determine both the portent's mechanics and its cryptic phrase. If one of the chosen dice is the d8, either recorded trigger can bring the portent due; when you invoke the portent, the triggering result that occurred is the result that applies. If more than one recorded trigger occurs at the same time, choose which triggering result applies.
If your foretold portent curdles, both readings of each chosen die are lost.

subclassFeature.name: Fulfilled Portent
entries: When your foretold portent is invoked, it becomes fulfilled until you finish your next long rest. A fulfilled portent can no longer be invoked.
While your portent is fulfilled, you can cast {@spell bestow curse} and {@spell remove curse} without expending a spell slot.
When you cast either spell in this way, the spell is cast at its lowest level, counts as an omen spell for you, and has a range of 30 feet.
When you finish a long rest, any fulfilled portent is lost before you make a new foretold portent.

subclassFeature.name: Rite of Servitude
entries: The Rite of Servitude is for those who would rather command the dead than commune with them. Practitioners of this rite call and bind spirits, settling them into prepared vessels and seating them in lifeless flesh to serve. Most cultures that know the rite call it taboo. The Shinjo, where the practice is best documented, exile its students.

subclassFeature.name: Rite Spells
entries: You learn the following spells at the listed occultist levels. They count as occultist spells for you and do not count against your spells known. If you already know one of these spells, choose another occultist spell of the same level from the necromancy or conjuration school.

entries.caption: Rite of Servitude Spells
rows: {@spell shadow of moil|XGE}, {@spell spirit of death|BMT}

subclassFeature.name: Soul Binder
entries: You have learned to bind a willing spirit into a gem and seat it in dead flesh.

Over the course of 1 hour, you perform a ritual in the presence of a spirit you can perceive or at a place where the boundary to the Border Ethereal is thin. At the end of the ritual, a willing spirit is bound into a gem worth at least 100 gp per the spirit's challenge rating, minimum 100 gp. The spirit's CR cannot exceed the maximum shown in the Soul Binding table.

As an action, you can seat the soul gem into the corpse of a creature you touch, animating it as your servant. The corpse must be the body of a Beast, Dragon, Fey, Giant, Humanoid, or Monstrosity. The corpse's CR must be equal to or lower than the soul gem's CR, and its size cannot exceed the maximum shown in the Soul Binding table. To animate the corpse, you expend spell slots with a combined level equal to the corpse's CR, minimum one 1st-level spell slot.

The servant uses the corpse's stat block, with the following changes: its type becomes Undead, its alignment becomes unaligned, and it loses any legendary actions, lair actions, and mythic actions. It is immune to poison damage and the poisoned condition, it does not require air, food, drink, or sleep, and it is immune to disease.

If the soul gem contains a soul that did not belong to the corpse in life, the servant is a foreign spirit. Its Intelligence becomes 3, its Wisdom becomes 6, and its Charisma becomes 5. It has no memories, languages, or personality from the corpse's life, and it obeys your commands. If one of the servant's statistics uses Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma, use the servant's new score to determine any relevant modifier, save DC, or attack bonus.

If the soul gem contains the soul that belonged to the corpse in life, the servant is a returned soul. It retains the creature's Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, memories, languages, personality, and other statistics. A returned soul is friendly to you and your companions, but it is not under your control. The DM controls the returned soul, which acts according to its own nature and judgment.

The servant rolls its own initiative. On each of your turns, you can use a bonus action to command a foreign spirit to move, attack, or take another action. If you issue no command, it takes the Dodge action. You can issue simple noncombat commands, such as "follow me" or "wait here," without using an action.
When the servant makes an ability check or saving throw that uses a proficiency bonus, it uses your proficiency bonus if yours is higher.

You can remove a seated soul gem from your servant over 1 minute while you are within 5 feet of it, causing the servant to become an inert corpse. If the servant is destroyed while the soul gem is seated in it, the gem is destroyed and the soul is released.
You can have only one soul gem and one active servant at a time. If you bind a new spirit, your previous soul gem is destroyed and the spirit within it is released.

subclassFeature.name: Lesser Soul Facets
entries: You learn to cut lesser facets into a soul gem. A soul gem can hold one lesser facet. During a 1-hour ritual with occult tools, you can cut or replace the lesser facet in a soul gem you possess that is not currently seated in a corpse. A soul gem cannot contain the same facet more than once.
Choose from the following lesser facets:

items: **Spirit Conduit.** Your bond with the servant runs in both directions while you and the servant are on the same plane. As a bonus action, you can perceive through the servant's senses until the start of your next turn. While doing so, you are blinded and deafened to your own surroundings. Once per turn, when your servant hits a creature with an attack, you can channel ethereal energy through the bond, dealing additional necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).
**Refined Attribute.** Choose one ability score. The servant's chosen score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.
**Grave-Hardened Hide.** The servant gains a +1 bonus to AC.
**Fortified Frame.** The servant's hit point maximum increases by twice your occultist level.
**Dead March.** The servant's walking speed increases by 10 feet.
**Corpse Grip.** The servant has advantage on ability checks made to grapple a creature or resist being shoved.
**Elemental Ward.** The servant gains resistance to one damage type of your choice other than bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing.
**Hollow Nerves.** The servant is immune to the frightened condition.
**Dead Flesh.** The servant has advantage on Constitution saving throws.
**Vicious Implements.** Once per turn, when the servant hits with a weapon attack or natural weapon attack, it deals extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
**Bone Hooks.** Once per turn, when the servant hits a creature with a melee attack, the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of the servant's next turn.
**Grave Weight.** Once per turn, when the servant hits a creature with a melee attack, the target cannot take reactions until the start of the servant's next turn.
**Silent Command.** You can communicate telepathically with the servant while it is within 120 feet of you. If the servant is a returned soul, it can respond telepathically.
**Easy Reseating.** When you re-seat this soul gem into a corpse, the spell slot levels required to animate the corpse are reduced by 1, to a minimum of one 1st-level slot.

subclassFeature.name: Greater Soul Facets
entries: Your soul gem can now hold two lesser facets and one greater facet. A soul gem can contain only one greater facet.
Choose from the following greater facets:

items: **Shared Vessel.** As a bonus action, while your servant is within 60 feet of you, you can transfer hit points between yourself and your servant in any combination, up to a total equal to your occultist level. Neither you nor the servant can exceed your respective hit point maximums, and you cannot transfer hit points to a servant whose current hit points are 0. You can use this facet a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest. In addition, you always know the direction and approximate distance to your active servant, even across planar boundaries.
**Imprinted Talent.** The animated creature gains one feat of your choice, provided it meets the feat's requirements.

**Eldritch Bond.** When you cast a spell with a range other than self, you can have the spell originate from the servant's space instead of yours, provided the servant is within 60 feet of you. You must still provide the spell's components, and you use your own spell attack bonus and spell save DC. If the servant has spellcasting, it can also have a spell it casts with a range other than self originate from your space, provided you are within 60 feet of it. The servant must still provide the spell's components, and it uses its own spell attack bonus and spell save DC.

**Incorporeal Passage.** The servant can move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes force damage equal to twice your proficiency bonus if it ends its turn inside an object.

**Soul-Snare.** Once per turn when the servant hits a creature with an attack, it can bind part of the target's spirit until the start of the servant's next turn. Until the effect ends, the target cannot teleport, become incorporeal, or willingly move to another plane.

**Borrowed Body.** As a bonus action, you can speak through the servant and perceive through its senses for 1 minute while it is within 120 feet of you. During this time, you are blinded and deafened to your own surroundings. If the servant is a returned soul, it can speak through you for the same duration. You can end this effect early as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Soul Gem Mastery
entries: Your soul gem can now hold three lesser facets and one greater facet.

In addition, you can become the soul that occupies your own prepared vessel. As an action while touching your seated soul gem, you transfer your soul into the gem. If another soul or spirit is bound in the gem, it is expelled to the Border Ethereal or its afterlife. Your body falls unconscious, becomes incapacitated, and enters a deathlike stasis until your soul returns.

While your soul occupies the gem, you control the servant's body as your own. You retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, memories, class features, proficiencies, and spellcasting, but use the servant's Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, size, speeds, senses, and physical traits. The body remains undead.

You can return to your body as an action, provided your body is on the same plane. When you do, the servant becomes an inert corpse. The soul gem remains intact, but it is empty and cannot animate a corpse until you bind another spirit into it.

If the soul gem or the servant's body is destroyed while your soul occupies the gem, your soul immediately returns to your own body. If your own body has been destroyed in the meantime, your soul departs to its afterlife as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Art of the Ether
entries: Practitioners of the Art of the Ether represent the scholarly magical path of thaumaphagic arts, where absorbed mana transforms into learned spell knowledge. Through intensive study and mental focus, these Thaumaphages learn to absorb the very patterns of spells cast at them, building a vast repertoire of magical knowledge through experience rather than traditional study.

subclassFeature.name: Mana Shaping
entries: Starting when you choose this art at 2nd level, when you are targeted by a spell, whether cast by a spellcaster or originating from a magic item, or an innate magical ability from a creature, you can use your reaction to attempt to understand and learn its magical signature. This attempt is made after the effects of the spell or ability have been resolved. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

To learn the spell or innate magical ability, you must make a Wisdom (Arcana) check. The DC for this check equals 10 + the spell's level x 2 or 10 + the creature's Challenge Rating, whichever is higher. For spells originating from a magic item, use the spell's level; if the item's effect is not a spell with a discrete level, the DM determines an equivalent spell level for the DC calculation.

If successful, you learn the spell or innate magical ability. You can cast learned spells using your thaumaphage spellcasting feature by expending the appropriate amount of mana. Learned innate magical abilities can be replicated by expending mana equal to what a spell of a similar power level would cost, determined by the DM and typically based on an equivalent spell level.

You can know a number of learned spells and abilities equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your Thaumaphage level, rounded down, minimum 1. If you attempt to learn a new one while at your maximum, you must choose one to forget.
This ability can be used to learn spells from spellcasters or magic items, or innate magical abilities from creatures. It cannot be used to learn effects from other environmental magical sources.

subclassFeature.name: Mana Overflow
entries: Beginning at 10th level, when you gain mana from any source that would cause you to exceed your maximum mana capacity, instead of losing the excess, you can immediately cast a spell you know without expending mana.
The spell must have a mana cost equal to or less than the excess mana you would have gained. You can use this feature once per long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Conversion
entries: At 14th level, you achieve a new height of control over magical energies, allowing you to convert your magical knowledge or existing spell energy into raw mana.

As a bonus action, you can choose one spell you know through your Mana Shaping feature and temporarily unlearn it until you finish a long rest. When you do so, you regain mana equal to half the spell's base mana cost, rounded down. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, minimum once, per long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Art of the Flesh
entries: Practitioners of the Art of the Flesh represent the physical path of thaumaphagic arts, where absorbed magical energy transforms into enhanced bodily capabilities. Inspired by ancient warrior traditions, these Thaumaphages learn to channel magical forces directly into their physical form, achieving superhuman strength, speed, and resilience. This art emerged from remote warrior-lodges in mountainous regions, where fighter-mystics sought to match the supernatural abilities of their enemies through magical absorption rather than traditional training.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior's Path
entries: Starting when you choose this art at 2nd level, your training focuses on physical combat enhanced by magical energy.
You gain proficiency in Athletics if you don't already have it. Additionally, you gain proficiency with either one martial weapon of your choice or shields.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Form
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can consume mana to convert your stored magical energy into temporary physical enhancements. These enhancements last for 1 hour and don't stack with magic items or spells that provide similar benefits.

You can only have one Mana Enhancement active at any given time. If you activate a new Mana Enhancement while another is active, the previous one ends. Enhanced ability score modifiers affect your statistics normally while active, but your enhanced ability scores cannot exceed 20 + your Constitution modifier, regardless of other bonuses.

items: {@b Strength Enhancement:} You can spend mana to enhance your Strength modifier. For every 2 mana you spend, you gain a +1 bonus to your Strength modifier. The maximum bonus you can gain from this enhancement is equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

{@b Dexterity Enhancement:} You can spend mana to enhance your Dexterity modifier. For every 2 mana you spend, you gain a +1 bonus to your Dexterity modifier. The maximum bonus you can gain from this enhancement is equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

{@b Constitution Enhancement:} You can spend mana to enhance your Constitution modifier. For every 2 mana you spend, you gain a +1 bonus to your Constitution modifier. The maximum bonus you can gain from this enhancement is equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

subclassFeature.name: Adaptive Surge
entries: Starting at 6th level, you unlock new ways to channel your mana for potent, immediate effects. You can use the following abilities by expending mana as specified:

items: {@b Kinetic Weave:} You can spend 1 mana as a bonus action to take the Dash or Disengage action.

{@b Resilient Hide:} You can use your bonus action and spend 2 mana to gain temporary hit points equal to your Thaumaphage level. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute or until they are replaced by new temporary hit points.

{@b Surging Might:} When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can choose to spend 2 mana to double the number of weapon damage dice rolled for that attack. This effect applies only to the weapon's base damage dice. You can use this option once per attack.

subclassFeature.name: Shared Vigor
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can channel your vitalizing mana to empower an ally. As an action, you can touch one willing creature and expend mana to grant them one of the benefits of your Empowered Form feature: Strength Enhancement, Dexterity Enhancement, or Constitution Enhancement.
The mana cost, duration of 1 hour, and maximum bonus of half your proficiency bonus are identical to when you use Empowered Form on yourself.

While an enhancement you granted is active on another creature via this feature, you cannot benefit from your own Empowered Form feature. Activating Empowered Form on yourself causes any enhancement you previously granted to another creature via this feature to end immediately. Similarly, granting an enhancement to a creature via this feature ends any Empowered Form currently active on yourself.

subclassFeature.name: Mind Over Flesh
entries: Beginning at 14th level, your mastery over mana allows you to sustain multiple augmentations with greater ease.

The restriction from Empowered Form that you can only have one enhancement active at a time is modified: you can now have multiple different Mana Enhancements, Strength, Dexterity, and/or Constitution, active simultaneously on yourself. You must pay the mana cost for each enhancement you activate and maintain on yourself.

Additionally, while you have one or more enhancements active on yourself, you can also maintain one enhancement on a single willing creature using your Shared Vigor feature. If you grant an enhancement to a different ally using Shared Vigor, the enhancement on the previous ally ends, but your personal enhancements remain unaffected.
The abilities granted by your Adaptive Surge feature can be used as described, by expending mana for their effects, regardless of which Mana Enhancements are active.

subclassFeature.name: Pale Touched
entries: Your magic is touched by the eerie energies of the Ethereal Plane, the boundary between life and death. This connection could be due to dramatic supernatural events, such as being stillborn and miraculously revived, having a powerful incorporeal undead as a relative, or experiencing a resurrection that brought a fragment of the Ethereal back with you. Your existence teeters between realms, drawing on the power of the Ethereal Plane to wield frightening and mysterious arcane forces. Spirits gravitate toward you, manipulated by your will.

subclassFeature.name: Deathly Eyes
entries: Your senses are finely attuned to the Ethereal Plane, allowing you to perceive and manipulate spirits to influence the battlefield. Your pupils emit a subtle, eerie glow, granting you Ethereal Sight up to 30 feet. You also often hear faint whispers and sense eerie presences from the Ethereal Plane.
This attunement allows you to detect the presence of incorporeal undead creatures, giving you advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks to detect them.

Additionally, when a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that roll, as the spirits disrupt their actions. Alternatively, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on a saving throw against an attack or effect you can see, as the spirits distract and unsettle your attacker.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. You can also spend 1 sorcery point to use this feature again before finishing a long rest.

entries.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: Ethereal Sight allows a creature to perceive the Ethereal Plane within a specified range. Unlike {@sense truesight}, Ethereal Sight is limited to detecting incorporeal undead and ghostly remnants of the dead that reside in the Ethereal Plane. Creatures with Ethereal Sight can see these entities and any other spectral phenomena within the range of their sight.

subclassFeature.name: Gift from the Veil
entries: Your link to the Ethereal Plane allows you to learn spells normally associated with the Occultist class. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn a sorcerer cantrip or a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose the new spell from the occultist spell list or the sorcerer spell list. You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you.

subclassFeature.name: Ghostwalk
entries: As a bonus action, you can become ethereal until the end of your turn. While ethereal, you can move through creatures and solid objects as if they were difficult terrain. If you end your turn inside an object, you take 1d10 force damage and are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. You can also spend 2 sorcery points to use this feature again before finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Haunting Strikes
entries: When you attack a creature with a spell attack, ghostly apparitions surge forth. On a successful hit, they deal an additional 2d6 psychic damage. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. You can also spend 2 sorcery points to use this feature again before finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Harrowing Tide
entries: You learn to summon a tide of tormented souls from the Ethereal Plane to engulf your foes, ripping their spirits from their bodies. As an action, you can choose up to three creatures you can see within 60 feet of you. Each target must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC.

On a failed save, a creature's spirit is forcefully taken to the depths of the misty shore, beyond the Border Ethereal, while its physical body collapses where it stands and is {@condition incapacitated}. At the end of each of the creature's turns, its spirit takes 3d6 necrotic damage as wailing souls assault it.

A creature affected by this feature can repeat the Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The effect also ends if the creature's incapacitated body is reduced to 0 hit points, or after 1 minute. When the effect ends, the creature's spirit instantly returns to its body. The creature must then succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest. Alternatively, you can spend 5 sorcery points to use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Shaman
entries: Shamans walk the boundary between the living world and the realm of spirits, channeling both primal fury and ethereal forces through their battle trance. These warriors bind their souls to the natural and spiritual worlds, communing with beast spirits and the dead alike. Unlike druids who commune with nature through contemplation, shamans channel primal and ethereal magic through fury and ecstasy. Their rage is not mindless; it is a deliberate trance state that opens doorways between worlds.

Through ritual tattooing, shamans inscribe magic upon their flesh: spells that flare to life when they enter their battle trance, drawing on both the raw vitality of nature and the psychic energies of the Border Ethereal. In this state, they can see through the eyes of distant beasts, speak with the spirits of the dead, and eventually transcend the boundary between flesh and spirit entirely.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: When you choose this path at 3rd level, you gain the ability to cast spells. See chapter 10 of the {@book Player's Handbook|PHB} for the general rules of spellcasting.

entries.name: Rune Tattoos
entries: You inscribe magical runes upon your flesh through ritual tattooing. These tattoos serve as permanent conduits for both primal and ethereal magic, each one representing a spell you have learned through ritual and meditation. Your rune tattoos function as your spellcasting focus.

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: You know three 1st-level spells of your choice from the occultist or druid spell lists. The Spells Known column of the Path of the Shaman Spellcasting table shows when you learn more spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. When you gain a level in this class, you can replace one shaman spell you know with another spell from the occultist or druid spell list.

When you learn a new spell, you must perform a ritual tattooing that takes 8 hours and consumes materials worth 50 gp per spell level. This ritual permanently inscribes a new rune tattoo upon your body.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Path of the Shaman Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your shaman spells, as your magic flows from spiritual awareness and attunement to both the natural world and the Border Ethereal. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a shaman spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
{@b Spell save DC} = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
{@b Spell attack modifier} = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

entries.name: Trance Casting
entries: While you are raging, you can cast your shaman spells, and your rage no longer prevents you from casting spells or maintaining concentration. Instead, your battle trance channels your fury into magical power. When you aren't raging, your rune tattoos lie dormant and cannot channel magic.
While raging, if you cast a shaman spell on your turn, you are considered to have attacked a hostile creature for the purpose of maintaining your rage.
When you cast a shaman spell that deals damage while raging, you add your rage damage bonus to one damage roll of that spell.

entries.name: Ritual Casting
entries: You can cast a shaman spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you know the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit Sight
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn to touch the souls of beasts, seeing through their eyes and hearing through their ears.

As an action, you can see through the eyes and hear through the ears of a beast you can see. The connection lasts for up to 10 minutes, and you must maintain concentration (as if concentrating on a spell).

items: You perceive through the beast's senses, not your own (you are blind and deaf to your own surroundings)
The beast is charmed by you for the duration
If the beast is unwilling, it can make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC at the start of each of its turns to end the effect

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1), regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit Bond
entries: At 6th level, you can form a permanent spiritual bond with a beast by deeply intertwining your soul with theirs.

entries.name: Bonding Ritual
entries: While using Spirit Sight on a willing beast, you can extend the connection into an 8-hour bonding ritual of continuous meditation and soul communion. You cannot take any other actions during this time, and if your concentration is broken, the ritual fails.

At the end of the 8 hours, make an {@skill Animal Handling} check with a DC equal to 10 + the beast's Wisdom score + the beast's CR (rounded down). On a success, the beast becomes your bonded companion and a fragment of your soul is permanently bound to theirs.

entries.name: Bond Limits and Transfer
entries: You can have only one bonded companion at a time. If you bond with a new beast, the previous soul fragment transfers to the new companion and the old bond ends. If your bonded companion dies, you can bond with a new beast.

entries.name: Companion Benefits
entries: Your bonded companion gains the following benefits:

items: Its hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to your {@class Barbarian|PHB} level x 3
It adds your proficiency bonus to its AC, attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws
When you finish a long rest, your bonded companion regains all its hit points

entries.name: Commanding the Bond
entries: Your bonded companion obeys your commands as best it can. It takes its turn on your initiative, though it doesn't take an action unless you command it to. On your turn, you can verbally command the beast where to move (no action required by you). You can use your bonus action to verbally command it to take the Attack, Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action.

subclassFeature.name: Versatile Casting
entries: At 10th level, your mastery of shamanic magic allows you to access your power outside of your battle trance.
You learn two cantrips of your choice from the occultist or druid spell list. You can cast these cantrips at any time.
Additionally, you can cast shaman spells of 1st or 2nd level even when you are not raging. Spells of 3rd level or higher still require you to be raging to cast.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit Vessel
entries: At 14th level, you achieve the ultimate expression of shamanic soul-binding: the ability to fully transfer your spirit into your bonded companion's body, becoming one with the beast.
As an action, your spirit leaves your body and takes control of your bonded companion. Your body falls unconscious (but stable) in whatever location you left it.

entries.name: While inhabiting your bonded companion
items: You retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores, as well as your personality, memories, and identity
You use the beast's Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, hit points, and physical features

You retain your class features and proficiencies, but can only use those that the beast's body can physically perform (for example, you can cast spells if the beast has a way to provide somatic and verbal components)
You can speak if the beast is capable of speech, otherwise you communicate through the beast's natural sounds

entries: You can return to your body as an action, provided you are on the same plane of existence as your body. Your spirit instantly returns to your body, and you awaken in it.

{@b If your bonded companion is reduced to 0 hit points while you inhabit it,} your spirit is violently forced back into your body. You immediately take {@dice 4d10} necrotic damage. If this damage reduces you to 0 hit points, you are dying.

{@b If your body is killed while your spirit inhabits your bonded companion,} your spirit becomes permanently bound to the beast. You continue to exist within your bonded companion with your mental ability scores, personality, and class features, but you can never return to humanoid form. You have become the beast, retaining only your mind and soul. The beast's original consciousness accepts your presence, and you share the body as co-inhabitants -- you control the body, but the beast's instincts and nature remain part of you.

spell.name: Spectral Weapon
entries: You summon a ghostly, ethereal one-handed weapon that manifests in your hand. As part of casting this spell, you make one melee spell attack against a creature within reach using this weapon. The weapon's form reflects aspects of your existence, tied to your ancestry, occupation, or personality, such as an ancestral blade, a spectral warrior's weapon from your past, or an ethereal representation of your inner spirit.

You have proficiency with the weapon, and it is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. You use your spellcasting ability for the attack roll. On a hit, the attack deals 2d4 necrotic damage. The spectral weapon disappears at the end of your turn.
This spell's damage increases by 2d4 when you reach 5th level (4d4), 11th level (6d4), and 17th level (8d4).

spell.name: Haunt
entries: You conjure a vengeful spirit to torment a creature within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be haunted by the spirit.

While haunted, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls for the duration of the spell. The target can make another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

spell.name: Dead Reckoning
entries: You touch a corpse and connect to its lingering essence, determining whether its spirit died unjustly and if it now resides in the Ethereal Plane.

If the creature's death was wrongful, such as through murder, betrayal, or foul play, you immediately sense this. Additionally, if the spirit is still present in the Ethereal Plane, you gain insight into whether it lingers due to unresolved business or if external forces trap it there.

spell.name: Spirit Lantern
entries: You conjure a ghostly light that hovers around you, shedding dim light in a 10-foot radius. This spectral illumination also grants Ethereal Sight within the same 10-foot radius.
The ghostly light remains in the same space as you, moving as you move.

As a bonus action, you can direct the ghostly light to attack a target within 60 feet. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 necrotic damage and 1d6 force damage. After the attack, the spell ends.

Ethereal Sight allows a creature to perceive the Ethereal Plane within the range. Unlike Truesight, Ethereal Sight is limited to detecting incorporeal undead and ghostly remnants of the dead that reside in the Ethereal Plane. Creatures with Ethereal Sight can see these entities and any other spectral phenomena within the range of their sight.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st. The additional dice are added to the necrotic damage.

spell.name: Detect Spirits
entries: You sense the presence of spirits and living creatures within 1,000 feet of you. You know the direction and distance to each one.
If a spirit or creature is bound to an object, place, or being, you sense that connection as well.

If the number of spirits and living creatures within range exceeds 10, you must make a Wisdom (Perception) check to discern individual presences. The DC equals 10 + 1 for every additional 5 creatures beyond the first 10. On a success, you discern individual spirits and their locations. On a failure, you sense only the general presence and total number of spirits but cannot distinguish them individually.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the range increases by 500 feet and the number of creatures you can sense without making a Wisdom (Perception) check increases by 5 for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Spectral Brand
entries: You mark a creature within range with an ethereal brand visible only to you and those who can see spirits.
While marked, the target has disadvantage on saving throws against your necromancy spells and takes 2d6 necrotic damage the first time it is hit by an attack each turn.

spell.name: Haunting Presence

components.m: a black feather and a small piece of bone

entries: You conjure a lingering spirit to haunt an area within a 30-foot radius centered on a point within range. Creatures entering the area must make a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of the haunting presence for the duration. While in the haunted area, creatures also have disadvantage on all saving throws to resist fear effects.
Additionally, any frightened creatures take 2d6 psychic damage at the start of each of their turns.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the psychic damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Blood Offering

components.m: a ceremonial dagger used to draw your blood

entries: You perform a profound blood ritual, sacrificing a portion of your own life force to empower another. As part of casting this spell, you expend any number of your own Hit Dice, up to a maximum equal to half your level, rounded down. Roll the expended Hit Dice and add your spellcasting ability modifier to the total.
One creature of your choice within range gains one of the following benefits, which you choose when you cast the spell:

items: Life Transference. The target regains hit points equal to the total rolled. You take necrotic damage equal to half of the hit points restored, rounded down. This damage cannot be reduced or prevented in any way.
Empowered Strength. The target gains a bonus to all attack rolls and damage rolls equal to +1 for each 5 points of the total rolled, minimum +1. This benefit lasts for 1 minute.
Resilient Will. The target gains a bonus to all saving throws equal to +1 for each 5 points of the total rolled, minimum +1. This benefit lasts for 1 minute.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the maximum number of Hit Dice you can expend increases by one for each slot level above 4th.

spell.name: Spectral Carriage

m.text: a silver coin and a black onyx gem worth 250 gp

entries: You summon an ethereal carriage driven by a spectral hackman, which appears within range. The carriage can seat up to 6 passengers and travels overland at 60 feet per round, ignoring difficult terrain.
The carriage can cross bodies of water as if on solid ground, but it cannot fly.
The carriage disappears after 8 hours or when you dismiss it as an action.

spell.name: Soulfire

components.m: a small crystal vial containing a drop of the caster's blood

entries: You sacrifice fragments of your own soul, igniting them into spectral flames that surge toward a creature within range. Roll 4d4; the result is the number of soul fragments you sacrifice.
Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes a number of d6s of necrotic damage equal to the number of soul fragments sacrificed.
Immediately after casting this spell, you take 1 necrotic damage for each soul fragment sacrificed.
On the target's next turn, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or take 1 necrotic damage for each soul fragment sacrificed.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the number of soul fragments sacrificed increases by 1d4 for each slot level above 4th.

spell.name: Wail of the Banshee

m.text: a shard of obsidian worth 100 gp

entries: You unleash a terrifying, otherworldly wail that echoes with the voices of the dead. Each creature of your choice within a 30-foot radius centered on you must make a Constitution saving throw.
On a failed save, the creature takes 6d6 psychic damage and is frightened of you for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and is not frightened.
A frightened creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Malediction

components.m: a length of black thread, which the spell consumes

entries: You speak a formal curse against one creature you can see within range, naming one ability: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.

If you possess a personal token from the target, such as a lock of hair, a drop of blood, a tooth, a written signature, or a cherished possession, the target need not be within range or visible to you, but it must be on the same plane of existence as you. A token used in this way is consumed by the spell.
The target must make a saving throw using the chosen ability. On a failed save, the target is cursed for the duration. On a successful save, the spell ends.

At dawn each day while cursed, the target repeats the saving throw using the chosen ability. On a failed save, that ability score is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 1. This reduction lasts until the curse ends.
The curse ends if removed by {@spell remove curse}, {@spell greater restoration}, or similar magic.

spell.name: Egregore

components.m: a personal token bearing a name, symbol, vow, or memory shared by more than one creature

entries: You draw together psychic residue, shared belief, and spiritual impressions into a visible thought-form in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The egregore is not a creature or object.
It cannot be attacked, damaged, healed, targeted, or moved against its will. It occupies no space, though its presence is visible and palpable.
The egregore fills a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on itself. When you cast the spell, choose one emotional aspect for the egregore: dread, grief, reverence, or guilt.

When a creature of your choice starts its turn in the sphere or enters it for the first time on a turn, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is affected by the egregore until the start of its next turn, according to the chosen aspect:

items: Dread. The creature is frightened of the egregore.
Grief. The creature's speed is halved, and it can't take reactions.
Reverence. The creature can't willingly attack you or your allies unless it first takes psychic damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.
Guilt. The creature has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures it damaged since the end of your last turn.

entries: As a bonus action on your turn, you can move the egregore up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see within range.
A creature that succeeds on its saving throw is immune to this casting of the spell until the start of your next turn.

spell.name: Call Spirit

components.m: a funerary token, cracked mirror, or personal object once carried by the dead

entries: You call a spirit from the Border Ethereal, causing it to manifest in an unoccupied space you can see within range. Choose one of the following spirits: a ghost, banshee, or wraith. The spirit uses the chosen creature's stat block.

For the duration, the spirit is friendly to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count, but takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands, requiring no action from you. If you issue no command, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid obvious danger.

When your concentration ends, your influence over the spirit ends. The spirit does not disappear. It becomes hostile to all creatures and is no longer bound to obey you. If the spirit is not already in combat, it rolls initiative.
If the spirit is possessing a creature when your concentration ends, the possession does not automatically end, but the spirit is no longer bound to obey you.

spell.name: Silvered Sanctum

m.text: a silver key and a black onyx worth 500 gp

entries: You touch a doorway, arch, gate, curtain, hatch, or other physical threshold leading into an enclosed room or chamber on the Material Plane. The room must fit within a 30-foot cube. For the duration, you cloister the room's reflection in the Border Ethereal, creating a hidden refuge called a silvered sanctum.

The sanctum has the same shape, boundaries, furnishings, and appearance as the chosen room had when you cast the spell, but it exists in the Border Ethereal rather than on the Material Plane. Objects created as part of the sanctum function normally inside it, but they dissolve into pale mist if removed.

You and any creatures you designate when you cast the spell can enter or leave the sanctum through the chosen threshold while within 5 feet of it. A permitted creature can bring objects through the threshold if those objects fit. Objects brought into the sanctum in this way are physically present there and can be used, stored, rearranged, or removed normally.

Creatures inside the sanctum can see and hear the Material room as faint, gray impressions, but can't affect it unless a feature or spell allows interaction across the planar boundary. Creatures in the Material room can't perceive creatures or objects inside the sanctum except by magic that reveals the Ethereal Plane.

The chosen threshold is the only stable passage between the sanctum and the Material room. If a creature or object leaves the sanctum through any other opening or boundary, it appears on the Material Plane in the corresponding space outside the chosen room, or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

The sanctum contains breathable air and a stable atmosphere. Spirits, incorporeal undead, and creatures on the Border Ethereal can't enter the sanctum unless you allow them. A creature inside the sanctum can't be possessed unless you allow it. Divination magic of 6th level or lower can't perceive into the sanctum or locate creatures or objects within it.

When the spell ends, creatures and objects brought into the sanctum appear in the nearest unoccupied spaces to the chosen threshold on the Material Plane. Objects created as part of the sanctum vanish.

entries.name: Permanent Sanctum
entries: If you cast this spell on the same threshold every day for 30 consecutive days, expending the material component each time, the sanctum becomes permanent. A permanent sanctum retains the shape and appearance it had when the final casting was completed, even if the Material room later changes, decays, or is destroyed.

You can have only one permanent silvered sanctum at a time. If you make another sanctum permanent, the previous one collapses, expelling its creatures and brought objects to the nearest unoccupied spaces to its threshold, or to the place where that threshold last existed.

spell.name: Spirit Conclave

m.text: a black candle infused with silver dust worth 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You summon a conclave of ethereal spirits to your side. Each spirit takes the form of a spectral warrior that acts as a phantom warrior under your control.
The number of spirits summoned is equal to half your spellcasting ability modifier, rounded down. The spirits remain for the duration of the spell or until they are reduced to 0 hit points.
The spirits act on the same initiative as you, taking their turn immediately after yours.

You can telepathically command all spectral warriors to move and attack as a bonus action on your turn. The spirits must be within 60 feet of you to receive commands. If no commands are issued, the spirits only take the Dodge action to defend themselves.

spell.name: Doom

components.m: a black thread knotted around a fragment of bone

entries: You pronounce a deathly curse upon one creature you can see within range. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target is doomed.

While doomed, the target is dying and makes death saving throws at the start of each of its turns, even if it is conscious and above 0 hit points. Death saving throws made this way are made with disadvantage. The target can otherwise act normally.

The spell ends if the target dies, succeeds on three death saving throws, or if the curse is removed by {@spell remove curse}, {@spell greater restoration}, or similar magic cast using a spell slot of 8th level or higher.

spell.name: Final Witness

components.m: a silvered mirror, a black candle, and a personal token, corpse, object, or piece of a location tied to the event

entries: You touch a corpse, remains, haunted object, personal token, or point in a location marked by death, betrayal, possession, a curse, a pact, a haunting, or a similar spiritually significant event. Name or describe the event or truth you seek.

The spell draws forth the strongest relevant spiritual impression bound to the target and reveals it to you as a vision lasting up to 10 minutes. The vision shows the event as impressed upon the Border Ethereal, including sights, sounds, words, visible magic, emotional residue, spiritual presences, and apparent actions.

The vision reveals only what was present in the event or impressed upon the target. It doesn't reveal thoughts, motives, secrets, or facts that left no spiritual impression. The DM determines what the vision shows.

Illusions, disguises, memory-altering magic, and magic that obscures divination don't deceive the vision unless created by a spell of 9th level or by {@spell wish}. If such magic obscures the event, you know the vision has been veiled.
If the target bears no meaningful spiritual impression tied to your request, the spell reveals that absence instead of producing a false vision.
Once you cast this spell on a particular corpse, remains, object, token, or location, you can't cast it on that same target again for 7 days.

spell.name: The Last Door

components.m: a key carved from bone and a black thread tied through its bow

entries: You open an invisible spiritual threshold centered on a point you can see within range. The threshold fills a 60-foot-radius sphere for the duration.

Within the sphere, you can see and hear into the Border Ethereal, and creatures there appear to you as translucent figures. Spirits, ghosts, incorporeal undead, and creatures on the Border Ethereal can't willingly enter or leave the sphere, move between the Material Plane and the Border Ethereal, pass through objects within the sphere, or possess a creature or object unless you allow it.

When you cast the spell, and again as an action on each of your turns, choose one creature, spirit, haunt, or possessing presence within the sphere that is possessing a creature or object or is present on the Border Ethereal.

The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it is expelled from any possession and appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within the sphere, either on the Material Plane or the Border Ethereal. On a success, it is unaffected.

If a creature dies within the sphere, its soul can't be trapped, consumed, transformed into undead, hidden from divination, or carried away by a spell or supernatural effect unless you allow it. You can also hold the soul at the threshold until the spell ends.

As an action, you can cause one willing dead soul whose body, remains, or personal token is within the sphere to appear as a visible apparition until the spell ends. The apparition can speak, but it can't cast spells, attack, or physically interact with the world. This manifestation doesn't restore the creature to life.

spell.name: Soul Injunction

components.m: a strip of black parchment bearing the target's name or likeness, sealed with wax

entries: You bind one creature you can see within range with a prohibition upon its soul. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, choose one injunction below. The target is bound by that injunction for the duration and knows its nature.

items: Bar the Crossing. The target can't willingly enter another plane of existence. If it casts a spell or uses a feature that would transport it to another plane, the effect fails for the target.
Forbid Possession. The target can't possess another creature or object, or willingly allow itself to be possessed.
Deny Return. If the target dies, it can't be restored to life for the duration.
Seal the Name. Choose one name, word, or phrase of up to ten words. The target can't knowingly speak it, write it, sign it, or communicate it by magical means.

Close the Threshold. Choose one structure, sanctum, graveyard, shrine, settlement district, or clearly bounded site known to you that fits within a 1,000-foot cube. The target can't willingly enter that place.
Expose the Soul. The target can't benefit from magic that would hide its soul, spirit, alignment, creature type, possession, curse, or spiritual attachments from divination magic.

entries: If the target attempts to violate the injunction, the attempt fails, and any action, bonus action, reaction, spell slot, or limited-use feature used in the attempt is wasted.
When you cast the spell, you can name a release condition. If the target fulfills that condition, the spell ends.
This spell can also be ended by {@spell remove curse}, {@spell greater restoration}, or similar magic cast using a 9th-level spell slot, or by {@spell wish}.

spell.name: The Unquiet Hour

components.m: a bell without a clapper and a handful of grave soil or ash from a burned home

entries: You awaken the fear, grief, guilt, and deathly residue in a location. Choose a point you can see within range. A 120-foot-radius sphere centered on that point becomes supernaturally haunted for the duration.

For the duration, the area is marked by cold shadows, bodiless whispers, and fleeting apparitions. While within the area, invisible creatures, spirits, incorporeal undead, possessing entities, and creatures on the Border Ethereal are visible to you. Curses, possessions, and spiritual attachments in the area also appear to you as supernatural marks.

When a creature of your choice starts its turn in the area, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is haunted until the start of its next turn. While haunted, the creature can't take reactions, its speed is halved, and it has disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened and against effects that curse, possess, or haunt it.
On a successful save, the creature is immune to this casting of the spell until the start of your next turn.
Spirits, ghosts, haunts, and incorporeal undead in the area can't become invisible to you or hide from you while on the Border Ethereal.
If such a creature ends a possession in the area, it remains visible to you until the start of your next turn.
The area remains marked by harmless supernatural signs for 24 hours after the spell ends.

spell.name: Convert Essence
entries: You touch a non-magical object and transmute your own vitality into raw magical potential, storing it within the item. You expend one of your Hit Dice, rolling it without regaining any hit points. The object gains a number of temporary magical charges equal to the number you rolled.

For the duration, any creature holding the object can use an action to draw on its power, expending any number of charges to gain temporary hit points. The number of temporary hit points gained is equal to the number of charges expended.
The charges fade after 1 minute or when all have been used. An object can only hold charges from one casting of this spell at a time.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The number of temporary hit points gained per charge increases as you gain levels. At 5th level, you gain 2 temporary hit points for each charge expended. This increases to 3 temporary hit points at 11th level, and 4 temporary hit points at 17th level.

spell.name: Ether Burn
entries: You unleash a blast of raw magic. Choose one of the following areas of effect originating from you: a 15-foot cone, a 10-foot radius sphere, or a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide.

Roll 4d6. The result is the spell's total force damage. This damage is divided evenly among all creatures within the chosen area, rounded down. Each affected creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, a creature takes half of its allocated share of the damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using additional mana, 2nd level or higher, the spell's total damage increases by 1d6 for each level above 1st.

item.name: Mana Crystal
entries: This crystal, often intricately carved or naturally formed with unique patterns, serves as a Thaumaphage's primary tool for storing and channeling magical energy. While attuned to this crystal, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your Thaumaphage spells.

The crystal has charges. The maximum number of charges the crystal can hold is equal to your current maximum mana capacity, as determined by your Thaumaphage class features. The crystal regains all its expended charges when you finish a long rest.
As an action, you can expend any number of charges from your Mana Crystal to gain an equal amount of mana, 1 charge = 1 mana, up to your maximum mana capacity.
If your Mana Crystal is lost or destroyed, you can craft a replacement during a long rest by expending 50 gp worth of materials and imbuing a suitable crystal with your own magical essence.

variantrule.name: Shadesilver Weapons
entries: These rules define base weapons made from Shadesilver. They do not define the additional powers of named magic items, hauntings, bound spirits, runes, or special forging.

entries.name: Rules Basis
entries: These rules use D&D 5e 2014 damage-type conventions.

items: Direct spirit contact, life drain, soul-wounding, withering, and incorporeal undead touch use necrotic damage.
Fear, madness, psionics, forced thoughts, forbidden knowledge, mental assault, and traumatic visions use psychic damage.

entries.name: Weapon Rules
entries: A weapon made of Shadesilver deals necrotic damage instead of its normal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage.
This is replacement damage, not bonus damage.
A Shadesilver weapon can pass through nonliving, nonmagical physical objects and barriers rather than striking them. It does not damage ignored objects by default.

The material does not grant sight through barriers. Attacks against creatures behind objects still require the wielder to perceive, locate, or correctly guess the target's space under normal unseen-target adjudication.

entries.name: Example: Shadesilver Longsword
entries: Damage: 1d8 necrotic, or 1d10 necrotic when wielded with two hands.
The weapon does not deal slashing damage unless another property changes its damage type.
The blade can pass through a mundane door, wall, shield, or other nonliving object, but the door, wall, or shield is not cut apart by that contact.

entries.name: Named Item Boundary
entries: Named Shadesilver weapons can have additional powers from enchantment, haunting, runes, bound spirits, or special forging. Those item-specific powers are not base Shadesilver weapon rules.

items: A haunted dagger can add hunger, psychic echoes, bonuses, or undead-specific effects.
An etherforged weapon can add stronger phasing or extra necrotic damage.

variantrule.name: Shadesilver Armor
entries: These rules define base armor made from Shadesilver. They do not define the additional powers of named magic items, hauntings, bound spirits, runes, or special forging.

entries.name: Rules Basis
entries: These rules use D&D 5e 2014 damage-type conventions.

items: Direct spirit contact, life drain, soul-wounding, withering, and incorporeal undead touch use necrotic damage.
Fear, madness, psionics, forced thoughts, forbidden knowledge, mental assault, and traumatic visions use psychic damage.

entries.name: Armor Rules
entries: Shadesilver armor grants no AC.
Shadesilver armor weighs one-tenth as much as a normal suit of armor of the same form.
Shadesilver armor is coincident armor: it anchors to the wearer's animating presence and does not occupy the same physical layer as mundane clothing or armor.
Because ordinary objects pass through it, it can be worn simultaneously with normal clothing or armor.
While wearing Shadesilver armor, the wearer has resistance to necrotic damage.

entries.name: Named Item Boundary
entries: Named Shadesilver armor can have additional powers from enchantment, haunting, runes, bound spirits, or special forging. Those item-specific powers are not base Shadesilver armor rules.

items: Soul-warded armor can add stronger protection against spirit contact.
Etherforged armor can add stronger phasing or specialized incorporeal defenses.

variantrule.name: Arcavene Weapons
entries: These rules define base weapons made from Arcavene. They do not define the additional powers of named Arcavene items.

entries.name: Shared Arcavene Enchantment Rules for Weapons
entries: Arcavene is a spell matrix material. A weapon made from Arcavene has no enchantment by default.
An unenchanted Arcavene weapon functions as a normal weapon of its ordinary type.
Arcavene enchantment works at the item level; the wielder's spell save DC and attack bonus do not apply by default.

items: Arcavene save DC is 12 + bound spell level.
Cantrip matrix DC is 12 (treated as spell level 0).
Arcavene uses a fixed item DC and fixed item spellcasting profile unless a named item says otherwise.
A named item may explicitly use the wielder's spell save DC or spell attack bonus, and must state that exception clearly.

entries: An enchanted Arcavene weapon always notes:

items: Bound spell and spell level.
Activation method.
Save DC or spell attack bonus when needed.
Attunement requirement.
Trigger, use limit, and reset condition.
Concentration handling.
Discharge behavior on use and after depletion.
Suppression and ending behavior.
Costly or consumed material components are supplied during enchantment.
Without an enchantment, Arcavene weapons have no Arcavene spell effect.
Enchanted Arcavene items require attunement unless the item entry says otherwise.

entries.name: Arcavene Weapon Rules
entries: Category: Weapon (rare or very rare).
No default attack bonus, damage bonus, extra damage, damage-type change, or additional passive benefit.
Uses ordinary weapon properties and base item statistics until enchanted.
Arcavene weapons can anchor Touch spells.
The wielder's hit against a creature is the contact for melee spell attacks.
For spells with saving throws, use the weapon's Arcavene save DC.
Spell targeting limits remain in force (for example, unwilling-target limits on self-only or willing-only effects stay enforced).

entries.name: Arcavene Weapon Trigger Process
entries: When an arcavene weapon has a valid bound spell and hits a creature:

items: Roll 1d10.
If the result is greater than the bound spell proc level, the bound spell triggers against the hit target.
If the result is less than or equal to the proc level, no spell effect occurs.

entries: There is no once-per-turn trigger limit.
Each hit can trigger a separate check.
The struck creature is the spell target.

entries.name: Cantrip Proc Levels
entries: Cantrips do not scale with wielder level once bound. They keep a fixed effect profile by effective caster level.
Cantrip spell level is still 0 for save DC math (12 + 0 = 12).
A 17th-level cantrip matrix (proc level 3) activates when 1d10 is higher than 3.

entries.name: Named Item Boundary
entries: Named Arcavene weapons can define additional named-item mechanics beyond these base rules, including altered activation conditions, altered proc levels, recharge behavior, and special targeting clauses.

variantrule.name: Arcavene Armor
entries: These rules define base armor made from Arcavene. They do not define the additional powers of named Arcavene items.

entries.name: Shared Arcavene Enchantment Rules
entries: Arcavene is a spell matrix material. Arcavene armor has no enchantment by default.
An unenchanted Arcavene armor behaves as normal armor of its listed type.
Enchanted Arcavene items require attunement unless the item entry says otherwise.

items: Arcavene uses a fixed item save DC and fixed item spellcasting profile.
Arcavene save DC = 12 + bound spell level.
Cantrip matrix DC = 12 (spell level 0).
Fixed item DC and attack values are used again on repeat checks unless the named item overrides.

entries: An enchanted Arcavene item must name:

items: Bound spell and spell level.
Activation method and trigger.
Save DC or spell attack bonus.
Attunement requirement.
Use limit and reset condition.
Concentration handling.
Discharge, suppression, and end behavior.
Costly or consumed material components are supplied when enchanting.
Without a bound spell, Arcavene items have no spell effect.

entries.name: Arcavene Armor Rules
entries: Category: Armor (rare or very rare).
No default enchantment benefits:

items: No AC bonus.
No resistance, damage-type change, weight reduction, or stealth benefit.

entries: Can anchor a Touch or Self spell that targets the wearer.
Duration spells can remain on the wearer while the armor is worn and the item remains attuned.
Instant spells can be triggered by events named in the item entry.

entries.name: Concentration Handling for Arcavene Armor
entries: If the bound spell requires concentration, the item entry must say whether:

items: The wearer maintains concentration, or
Arcavene matrix maintains concentration (this is allowed as a dangerous option).

entries: If the wearer drops concentration, concentration-maintained by the wearer ends immediately and cannot be assumed to persist.
Matrix-maintained concentration ends when attunement is broken, the item is removed, or an explicit suppression condition triggers.

entries.name: Named Item Boundary
entries: Named Arcavene armor can add restrictions and special interactions, including alternate triggers, self-only matrices, and nonstandard concentration behavior.

variantrule.name: Arcavene Rings
entries: These rules define Arcavene rings and provide the base enchantment model for ring-specific spell matrices. They do not define the additional powers of named Arcavene items.

entries.name: Shared Arcavene Ring Enchantment Rules
entries: Arcavene is a spell matrix material. Arcavene rings have no enchantment by default.
An unenchanted Arcavene ring functions as a normal ring of its kind.
Enchanted Arcavene items require attunement unless the item entry says otherwise.

items: Arcavene uses a fixed item save DC and fixed item spellcasting profile.
Arcavene save DC = 12 + bound spell level.
Cantrip matrix DC = 12.
The same DC applies on repeated uses unless the item says otherwise.
An enchanted Arcavene item must name:
Bound spell and spell level.
Activation method and trigger.
Save DC or spell attack bonus.
Attunement requirement.
Use limit and reset condition.
Concentration handling.
Discharge, suppression, and end behavior.
Costly or consumed material components are supplied during enchantment.
Without a bound spell, there is no Arcavene spell effect.

entries.name: Arcavene Ring Rules
entries: Category: Ring (rare or very rare).
Functions as a normal ring unless enchanted.
Can anchor Self or Touch spells that affect the wearer.
Duration spells can be maintained on the wearer while the ring is worn and the wearer is attuned.

entries.name: Concentration Handling
entries: If the named spell uses concentration, the item entry must specify whether:

items: Wearer-held concentration, or
Arcavene matrix-maintained concentration.

entries.name: Named Item Boundary
entries: Named Arcavene rings can define additional triggers, cooldowns, and targeted effects.

variantrule.name: Arcavene Wondrous Items
entries: These rules define Arcavene wondrous items used as amulets, circlets, masks, bracers, cloaks, shackles, and similar equipment classes. They do not define the additional powers of named Arcavene items.

entries.name: Shared Arcavene Wondrous-Item Enchantment Rules
entries: Arcavene is a spell matrix material. Arcavene wondrous items have no enchantment by default.
An unenchanted Arcavene wondrous item functions as a normal item.
Enchanted Arcavene items require attunement unless the item entry says otherwise.

items: Arcavene uses a fixed item save DC and fixed item spellcasting profile.
Arcavene save DC = 12 + bound spell level.
Cantrip matrix DC = 12.
The same DC is used for repeated checks unless the item entry changes it.
An enchanted Arcavene item must name:
Bound spell and spell level.
Activation method and trigger.
Save DC or spell attack bonus.
Attunement requirement.
Use limit and reset condition.
Concentration handling.
Discharge, suppression, and end behavior.
Costly or consumed material components are supplied during enchantment.
Without a bound spell, there is no Arcavene spell effect.

entries.name: Arcavene Wondrous-Item Rules
entries: Category: Wondrous item (rare or very rare).
Functions as a normal wondrous item unless enchanted.
Can anchor Self or Touch spells that affect the wearer.
Duration spells can be maintained while the item is worn and the wearer is attuned.

entries.name: Concentration Handling
entries: If the bound spell requires concentration, the item entry must specify whether the:

items: wearer maintains concentration, or
Arcavene matrix maintains concentration.

entries.name: Named Item Boundary
entries: Named Arcavene wondrous items can define wearer-specific targets, additional triggers, and alternate charge cycles.
